registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_acm_production_fuse
registers.description: Indicates if Authentication Code Module (ACM) is marked production of not

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_acm_production_worthy_fuse
registers.description: This fuse is set to indicate that the part is worthy of Production handling with regards to poison to PCH.

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_anchor_cove_en_fuse
registers.description: The Anchor Cove Enable fuse controls whether or not secure boot policy restrictions read from PCH will be followed (1=enable, 0=disable). Reset ucode mirrors the fuse in CORE_CR_ARCH_FUSES_ANCHOR_COVE_EN for microcode usages

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_arr_iec_fpc_parity_enable_fuse
registers.description: When this is set, it will enable ARR, IEC and FPC paritiy.

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_auto_cc6_feature_en_fuse
registers.description: Enables ucode support for Autonomous Core C6.

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_core_pqos_enf_enable_fuse
registers.description: CMT 14012759449: Memory BW Throttle control at the core.

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_cram_size_1024kb_fuse
registers.description: When this fuse is set, the patch CRAM/MCHECK will be sized up to 1 MB.

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_dam_check_enable_fuse
registers.description: TNT f1706667627 / b1706671069 - When this is set, only allow loading a debug patch with Delayed Authentication Mode (DAM)

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_favored_core_enable_fuse
registers.description: When this is set, disable the Intel Turbo Boost Max Technology 3.0

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_fred_enable_fuse
registers.description: Enable FRED instructions

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_fzm_disable_fuse
registers.description: This fuse is used to control (1=enable, 0=disable) Fast Zero Memory (FZM)/2LM feature

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_guest_timer_disable_fuse
registers.description: Disables the Guest Timer if set to 1 - VMCS PLA Color

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_hlat_en_fuse
registers.description: This fuse enables HLAT (Hypervisor-managed Linear Address Translation) feature - VMCS PLA Color 5

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_hwp_enable_fuse
registers.description: This fuse is used to enable Intel Hardware P-state 9HWP) feature. Note that TNT uses this one fuse in place of 4 Sunnycove fuses: HWP, HWP_INTERRUPT, HWP_THREAD_REQUEST_VALID_BITS_ENABLE and HWP_HIGHEST_CHANGE_NOTIFICATION

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_hwp_request_ignore_for_idle_lps_enable_fuse
registers.description: When this enable is 1, cpuid reports to software the ability to ignore HWP requests for idle processors.

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_lass_en_fuse
registers.description: This fuse is used to control (1=enable, 0=disable) Linear Address Space Separation (LASS)

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_ljpll_fusecr_afs_1_afs_dd0_comp_offset_fuse
registers.description: AFS droop detector 0 comparator offset; 0x0: max negative offset (-22mV) ; 0x8 no offset (0mV) ; 0xF: max positive offset (+19.4mV). 2.78mV per code

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_ljpll_fusecr_afs_1_afs_dd1_comp_offset_fuse
registers.description: AFS droop detector 1 comparator offset; 0x0: max negative offset (-22mV) ; 0x8 no offset (0mV) ; 0xF: max positive offset (+19.4mV). 2.78mV per code

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_ljpll_fusecr_afs_1_afs_dd2_comp_offset_fuse
registers.description: AFS droop detector 2 comparator offset; 0x0: max negative offset (-22mV) ; 0x8 no offset (0mV) ; 0xF: max positive offset (+19.4mV). 2.78mV per code

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_ljpll_fusecr_afs_1_afs_dd3_comp_offset_fuse
registers.description: AFS droop detector 3 comparator offset; 0x0: max negative offset (-22mV) ; 0x8 no offset (0mV) ; 0xF: max positive offset (+19.4mV). 2.78mV per code

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_ljpll_fusecr_afs_1_afs_dd_comp_thresh_fuse
registers.description: AFS droop detector comparator A threshold (higher threshold): 0x0 = minimum, 0x3F = maximum

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_ljpll_fusecr_afs_1_afs_delta_f_fuse
registers.description: PLL AFS frequency shift magnitude configuration: 0x0 = minumum, 0x7 = maximum

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_ljpll_fusecr_afs_1_afs_force_pfdchop_fuse
registers.description: Enable AFS sensitive PLL band-width by forcing the smallest PFD chop setting during droop event: 0x0 = disabled, 0x1 = enabled

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_ljpll_fusecr_afs_1_afs_mode_fuse
registers.description: AFS entry/exit mode in async mode; 0x0 = Instant Entry Instant Exit (IEIE), 0x2 = Instant Entry Gradual Exit (IEGE)

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_ljpll_fusecr_afs_1_afs_vid_boost_disable_fuse
registers.description: AFS VID boost feature disable; 0x0=feature enabled, 0x1=feature disabled

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_ljpll_fusecr_app_0_freq_throttle_delta_f_no1p5_fuse
registers.description: Disable frequency recovery step using divide-by-1.5. Encoding: 0x0 = recovery includes 1.5x, 0x1 = recovery skips 1.5x

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_ljpll_fusecr_app_0_freq_throttle_delta_f_ovrdval_fuse
registers.description: Interface override value for freq_throttle_delta_f - magnitude of frequency reduction during throttling request relative to nominal target frequency. Encoding: 0x0 = 1.5x, 0x1 = 2x, 0x2 = 4x, 0x3 = 8x

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_ljpll_fusecr_app_0_freq_throttle_delta_t_fuse
registers.description: Latency of each step in frequency during recovery from throttling. Encoding: 0x0 = minimum latency, 0x7 = maximum latency

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_ljpll_fusecr_dcc_0_dcs_offset_dca_static_fuse
registers.description: Duty cycle offset target with DCS enabled; static duty cycle setting with DCS enabled; 0x00=minimum high phase, 0x20=center setting, 0x3F=maximum high phase

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_ljpll_fusecr_fll_perf_0_det_lock_count_fuse
registers.description: Deterministic lock time duration defined as number of linear search measurements after binary search completion. Does not apply to pre-calibration or dynamic frequency changes. Encoding: 0x0 = 0, 0x1 = 4, 0x2 =8 , 0x3 = 16.

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_ljpll_fusecr_fll_perf_0_gv_afc_damping_fuse
registers.description: AFC GV ramp rate damping and proportional step size in unconstrained ramp rate mode. Encoding: 0x0 = lowest damping (Err/2), 0x1 = lower damping (Err/4), 0x2 = higher damping (Err/8), 0x3 = highest damping (Err/16).

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_ljpll_fusecr_fll_perf_0_gv_afc_exit_err_fuse
registers.description: AFC GV frequency error exit criteria relative to target count with offset given by gv_target_offset fuse. Encoding: 0x0 = lower error (up to 3 DCO codes), 0x1 = higher error (up to 7 DCO codes).

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_ljpll_fusecr_fll_perf_0_gv_afc_ramp_rate_fuse
registers.description: AFC GV frequency ramp rate in approximate GHz/us slope. Encoding: 0x0 = unconstrained, 0x1 = 0.25, 0x2 = 0.33, 0x3 = 0.45, 0x4 = 0.60, 0x5 = 0.75, 0x6 = 0.90, 0x7 = 1.1, 0x8 = 1.4, 0x9 = 1.8, 0xA = 2.3, 0xB = 2.9, 0xC = 3.6, 0xD = 4.7, 0xE = 6.6, 0xF = 9.4

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_ljpll_fusecr_fll_perf_0_raw_lock_thresh_fuse
registers.description: Frequency based lock detector threshold defined as allowed percentage error of target frequency count. Encoding: 0x0 = 0.75%, 0x1 = 1.5%, 0x2 = 3.0%, 0x3 = 6.0%.

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_ljpll_fusecr_fll_proc_0_dco_ireftune_fuse
registers.description: FLL DCOA IREF strength. Encoding: 0x0 = minimum strength, 0xF = maximum strength.

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_ljpll_fusecr_fll_proc_0_dco_settle_time_fuse
registers.description: Duration allocated to DCO settling after applying a code update prior to starting next measurement. Encoding: 0x0 = 10ns, 0x1 = 15ns, 0x2 = 20ns, 0x3 = 30ns, 0x4 = 35ns, 0x5 = 40ns, 0x6 = 50ns, 0x7 = 65ns.

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_ljpll_fusecr_fll_proc_0_gv_target_offset_fuse
registers.description: Frequency counter target offset to avoid overshoot during GV. FGV encoding: 0x0 = -1 count, 0x1 = -2 counts, 0x2 = -3 counts, 0x3 = -4 counts. AFC (SGV) encoding: 0x0 = -1 count, 0x1 = -3 counts, 0x2 = -5 counts, 0x3 = -7 counts.

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_ljpll_fusecr_fusa_0_fll_lockthresh_postlock_fuse
registers.description: Frequency based FLL lock detector threshold for FuSa defined as allowed percentage error of target frequency count. Encoding: 0x0 = 0.75%, 0x1 = 1.5%, 0x2 = 3.0%, 0x3 = 6.0%.

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_ljpll_fusecr_fusa_0_fusa_unlockerr_cfg_fuse
registers.description: FuSa unlock error indicator feature enable and reset condition: 0x0=disabled, 0x1=reset on side_rst, 0x2=reset on PLL disable, 0x3=reset on power-good de-assertion

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_ljpll_fusecr_fusa_0_pll_lockthresh_postlock_fuse
registers.description: Post-lock PLL lock detector phase error threshold above which PLL is considered unlocked. Encoding: 0x0 = 40ps, 0x1 = 70ps, 0x2 = 100ps, 0x3 = 130ps, 0x4 = 170ps, 0x5 = 200ps, 0x6 = 240ps, 0x7 = 270ps, 0x8 = 500ps, 0x9 = 800ps, 0xA = 1000ps, 0xB = 1300ps, 0xC = 1550ps, 0xD = 1800ps, 0xE = 2050ps, 0xF = 2300ps

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_ljpll_fusecr_perf_0_cpi_opamp_enable_fuse
registers.description: cpi opamp disable. Encoding: 0x0 = disabled, 0x1=enabled.

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_ljpll_fusecr_perf_0_ldo1_rcomp_cfg_fuse
registers.description: LDO 1 compensation resistor configuration: 0x0 = 1kOhm, 0x1 = 2kOhm, 0x2 = 3kOhm, 0x3 = 4kOhm.

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_ljpll_fusecr_proc_0_ldo_vrefsel_fuse
registers.description: LDO VREF trim configuration: 0x0 = minimum, 0x9 = maximum, 0xA-0xF = unused

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_ljpll_fusecr_proc_0_vco_trim_pg_fuse
registers.description: VCO RO powergate trim affecting VCO gain: 0x0 = min (6GHz/V), 0xF = max (26GHz/V)

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_mktme_en_fuse
registers.description: This fuse is used to control (1=enable, 0=disable) Multi-Key TME - VMCS PLA Color

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_physical_address_size_fuse
registers.description: This fuses established Physical Address size for the CPU. See enum for supported encodings

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_afs_vsense3_sel_ext_fuse
registers.description: Select source of 'afs_vsense[3]'

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_async_mode_fuse
registers.description: Gasket Async FIFO enable

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_async_s1align_freq_fuse
registers.description: Static fuse to set S1-align frequency

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_auto_c6_en_fuse
registers.description: Auto C6 is enabled. There is a parallel ucode fuse (AUTO_CC6_FEATURE_EN) and the value of this fuse and the ucode fuse should be the same.

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_biu_bgf_bypass_separation_fuse
registers.description: Same functional purpose as FB_PMA_BIU_BGF_SEPARATION_FUSE but for the PLL BYPASS MODE.

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_biu_bgf_separation_fuse
registers.description: To define the separation number in ns for the BGF during PLL FUNCTIONAL MODE using this formula:  Separation = Fuse_setting * 60/256.   Example:  value 15 =  15 * 60ns / 256 = 3.5156 ns. These values are only used when BGF mode is used (FB_ASYNC_MODE == 0)

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_boot_vid_fuse
registers.description: boot vid for module/l2 if applicable in 2.5mV encoding

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_clk_bypass_sel_fuse
registers.description: Survivability hook to allow ckblspine to select other clock sources for mclk when cl_biu_robypassen or cl_core_robypass_en[3:0] is set.

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_cri_trdy_timeout_fuse
registers.description: use to select timeout cycle counts in CRI transactions. Default value of FB_CRI_TIMEOUT_FUSE should be 3 (64 sbclks),  SOC's which use on-die FIVR should set this fuse to 0 (7 sbclk), SOC's which use off-die IVR (eg. FastLDO in RYF) should set this fuse based on the latency to offdie accesses.

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_crystal_freq_fuse
registers.description: crystal speed, used to gen 10us beat rate.

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_doma_en_fuse
registers.description: DOMA is enabled

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_dt_cy_timeout_fuse
registers.description: Dynamic duty cycle compensation max cycles timeout value; Fuse value is number of cycles

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_dts_tele_en_fuse
registers.description: To control DTS telemetry sampling and pushing at PMA. It has no effect on the actual DTS IP

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_dynamic_mclk_biu_comp_fuse
registers.description: The clock branches for Core0, Core1, Core2, Core3, L2, and BIU have an identical adjustable delay element inserted. Table 3(TODO) details the delay times for each fuse setting and is broken into two sections: coarse and fine adjustment. Coarse adjustments are made with the three most significant bits and fine adjustments with the four least significant bits. A one bit increment will cause the delay to increase by the amount shown. These values are only relevant if the dynamic behavior of all the core compensators is disabled by setting the fuse FB_DYNAMIC_MCLK_CORE_COMP_DISABLE_FUSE. Note that the TAP Clock Compensation Register supports a 7-bit input that will override the fuse value. See the TNT TAP High-Level Architecture Specification for information about the TAP interface.

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_en_auto_comp_for_evictions_fuse
registers.description: Fuse to enable/disable several features in BUS.  These features are hard to patch if we have a bug. 1.- The auto comp during BUS datapull from MEC for L1 evictions (additional CREG bit can fine-tune). Fuse also used to control whether to enable l2q full indcation to MEC.  2.- l2q Per Core Per Bank FULL  3.- Sending spec_val to the cored 4. - bank hash feature in DKT

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_en_dcu_parity_fuse
registers.description: Fuse to enable DCU Data Parity checking

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_enable_sbclk_reqack_fuse
registers.description: Default 0 : SBCLK_req/ack from PMA is disabled,1: SBCLK req/ack handshake from PMA is enabled and allows dynamic SBCLK gating by the SOC

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_enable_uclk_reqack_fuse
registers.description: Default 0 : uclk_req/ack from PMA is disabled and uclk_req should always be asserted from PMA to FIL,1: uclk req/ack handshake from PMA is enabled and allows dynamic UCLK gating by the SOC

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_fast_gv_fuse
registers.description: fast gv enable

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_fivr_tele_en_fuse
registers.description: To control FIVR telemetry sampling and pushing at PMA. It has no effect on the actual FIVR IP

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_l2_creg_spares_fuse
registers.description: BIT 0 is used for SWITCH_TO_ERI_MAILBOX_BEFORE_FIT_PATCH. Reserved goes to EBL_CR_FUSE_SPARE.FUSES creg

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_l2_number_slices_fuse
registers.description: Number of slices -- each slice has l2 sets equal to FB_PMA_L2_NUMBER_SETS_FUSE

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_l2_number_ways_fuse
registers.description: Fuse the number of L2 ways, 0-16 ways

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_l2_pwrmux_prim_en_fuse
registers.description: This Fuse will be used to disable the primary leg of l2pwrmux when there is a dedicated DVFS supply for vccpwrmuxalt_nom 0  Primary Leg disabled Power-gate only topology through the vccpwrmuxalt_nom 1 Primary Leg enabled Power-Mux feature supported

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_l2_self_timed_thresh_fuse
registers.description: Specifies the ratio in 100MHz encoding above which self timed feature is enabled. Shared ownership with araman1

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_l2_sleep_disable_ratio_thresh_fuse
registers.description: Specifies the ratio in 100MHz encodingÂ belowÂ which the sleep feature is disabledÂ to prevent retention issues for single-rail support. Shared ownership with araman1

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_l2_voltage_mux_equal_vid_fuse
registers.description: l2_voltage_mux_equal_vid is 11 bit threshold fuse. This is a value is milivolt/volts meaning the number programmed in this fuse will be multiplied by 2.5 mv to come up with a voltage value for the fuse. For example value 11'h000 is 0mv, 11'h001 is 2.5 mV, 11'h104 is 650mV. Programmed to the equalization voltage between the two supplies. The mux is low resistance on each leg, so to limit short circuit current voltage on the variable supply will be ramped to the equalization vid before switching. This is set to the value of the static supply. Reset default is 0x0, but the value is effectively NA because the threshold will prevent any switching when both of these fuses are not programmed

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_l2c6_slp_strength_en_fuse
registers.description: This fuse enables L2C6 bitcell sleep for power reduction ONLY during retention state. There is no L2C6 sleep capability in C0 state.

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_l2c6_slp_strength_fuse
registers.description: This is a bitcell sleep stack setting fuse for l2c6subm. All stacks ON set to 111

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_l2dat_clkshaper_saen_fuse
registers.description:
This fuse is used for the senseamp (SA) clock shapers for l2datsubm.
000: No SAEN pushout
001: Push SAEN rise and fall by 2 inversions
010: Push SAEN rise and fall by 4 inversions
011: Push SAEN rise and fall by 6 inversions
100: No SAEN pushout.
101: Push SAEN rise by 2 inversions
110: Push SAEN rise by 4 inversions
111: Push SAEN rise by 6 inversions

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_l2dat_clkshaper_selftimed_fuse
registers.description: Self timed SA shaper. 0,4,8,12 inversion pushout

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_l2dat_en_array_slpc0c2_fuse
registers.description: This fuse enables bitcell sleep during C0/C2 state for l2datsubm. To enable during C0/C2 state, FB_PMA_L2DAT_SLP_STRENGTH_EN_FUSE (l2datsubm) should also be 1. Bitcell sleep disabled in C0/C2 set to 0

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_l2dat_en_bl_float_fuse
registers.description: This fuse enables bitline float during all C* states for l2datsubm. To enable during C0/C2 state, FB_PMA_L2DAT_EN_BL_FLOATC0C2_FUSE should also be 1. Bitline float enabled = 1. Bitline float disabled = 0

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_l2dat_en_bl_floatc0c2_fuse
registers.description: This fuse enables bitline float during C0/C2 state for l2datsubm.  To enable during C0/C2 state, FB_PMA_L2DAT_EN_BL_FLOAT_FUSE (for l2datsubm) should also be 1. Bitline float disabled in C0/C2 set to 0

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_l2dat_en_diswaypwrdn_fuse
registers.description: This fuse enables dynamic/physical way powerdown for l2datsubm. Dynamic/physical way powerdown disabled set to 0

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_l2dat_en_dynwaydis_bl_float_fuse
registers.description: Enable BL Float for L2datsubm during dynamic waydisable. BL Float is automatically enabled for physical waydisable (i.e. ways which have been disabled by the FB_PMA_L2_NUMBER_WAYS_FUSE will have BLs floated)

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_l2dat_en_sa_float_fuse
registers.description: This fuse enables sense amp rddata/rddata_b float during all C* states for l2datsubm. To enable during C0/C2 state, FB_PMA_L2DAT_EN_SA_FLOATC0C2_FUSE should also be 1. Sense amp float enabled = 1. Sense amp float disabled = 0

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_l2dat_en_sa_floatc0c2_fuse
registers.description: This fuse enables sense amp float during C0/C2 state for l2datsubm.  To enable during C0/C2 state, FB_PMA_L2DAT_EN_SA_FLOAT_FUSE (for l2datsubm) should also be 1. Sense amp float disabled in C0/C2 set to 0

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_l2dat_en_wlslp_fuse
registers.description: This fuse enables wordline sleep during all C* states for l2datsubm. Wordline sleep enabled set to 1. Wordline sleep disabled set to 0

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_l2dat_slp_strength_en_fuse
registers.description: This fuse enables bitcell sleep during all C* states for l2datsubm. To enable during C0/C2 state, FB_PMA_L2DAT_EN_ARRAY_SLPC0C2_FUSE should also be 1. Bitcell sleep enabled = 1. Bitcell sleep disabled = 0

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_l2dat_slp_strength_fuse
registers.description: This is a bitcell sleep stack setting fuse for l2datsubm. All stacks ON set to 111

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_l2dat_sram_fuse
registers.description:
bit 7:5 spare
bit 4   wr_st_disable to force writes to be synch
bit 3   gatecap enable for left side of IO
bit 2   gatecap enable for right side of IO
bit 1:0 nbl kick delay 11,10,01,00 for 6,4,2,0 inv delay

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_l2dat_wl_bias_fuse
registers.description:
This is a wordline underdrive setting fuse for l2datsubm.
(Bits 1 to 0)
00: No WL underdrive (read assist)
01: Weakest WL underdrive setting (read assist)
10: 2nd weakest WL underdrive setting (read assist)
11: Strongest WL underdrive setting (read assist)
(Bit 2 is no con)

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_l2dat_wl_bias_step_en_fuse
registers.description:
This fuse enables the stepped wordline feature. Stepped wordline enabled set to 1. Stepped wordline disabled set to 0.
The stepped wordline bias enable overrides the wordline bias setting in the 2nd and 3rd phase (if available) of the wordline if the stepped wordline is enabled.
The 1st phase will still use the normal wordline bias enable. If the stepped wordline bias is disabled, then the normal wordline bias setting will be applied to all phases of the wordline.

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_l2dat_wr_ast_en_fuse
registers.description: This is the disable write assist fuse setting for l2datsubm. Write Assist enable set to 1. Write Assist disable set to 0

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_l2dat_wr_ast_pw_fuse
registers.description:
This is a write assist pulse width setting fuse for l2datsubm.
(Bits 3 to 2 are nocon)
XX00: Short self-timed pulse for write assist
XX01: Long self-timed pulse for write assist
XX1X: Synchronous self-timed pulse for write assist

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_l2dat_wr_ast_strength_fuse
registers.description:
This fuse is used for write assist P clamp settings.
(Bit 2)
0: disable coupling(kick) for gate cap
1: enable coupling(kick) for gate cap
(Bit 1)
0: disable coupling for large wire cap
1: enable coupling for large wire cap
(Bit 0)
0: disable coupling for medium wire cap
1: enable coupling for medium wire cap

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_l2tag_clkshaper_saen_fuse
registers.description:
This fuse is used for the senseamp (SA) clock shapers for l2tagsubm/l2statesubm.
000: No SAEN pushout
001: Push SAEN rise and fall by 2 inversions
010: Push SAEN rise and fall by 4 inversions
011: Push SAEN rise and fall by 6 inversions
100: No SAEN pushout.
101: Push SAEN rise by 2 inversions
110: Push SAEN rise by 4 inversions
111: Push SAEN rise by 6 inversions

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_l2tag_clkshaper_selftimed_fuse
registers.description: Self timed SA shaper. 0,4,8,12 inversion pushout

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_l2tag_en_bl_float_fuse
registers.description: This fuse enables bitline float during C3/C6 state for l2tagsubm/l2statesubm. Bitline float enabled during C3/C6 set to 1. Bitline float disabled during C3/C6 set to 0

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_l2tag_en_sa_float_fuse
registers.description: This fuse enables sense amp float during C3/C6 state for l2tagsubm/l2statesubm. Sense amp float enabled during C3/C6 set to 1. Bitline float disabled during C3/C6 set to 0

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_l2tag_en_wlslp_fuse
registers.description: This fuse enables wordline sleep during C3/C6 states for l2tagsubm/l2statesubm. Wordline sleep enabled during C3/C6 set to 1. Wordline sleep disabled during C3/C6 set to 0

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_l2tag_sram_fuse
registers.description:
bit 7:5 spare
bit 4   wr_st_disable to force writes to be synch
bit 3   gatecap enable for left side of IO
bit 2   gatecap enable for right side of IO
bit 1:0 nbl kick delay 11,10,01,00 for 6,4,2,0 inv delay

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_l2tag_wl_bias_fuse
registers.description:
This is a wordline underdrive setting fuse for l2tagsubm/l2statesubm.
(Bits 2 to 0)
000: No WL underdrive (read assist)
001: Weakest WL underdrive setting (read assist)
010: 2nd weakest WL underdrive setting (read assist)
011: Intermediate WL underdrive setting (read assist)
110: 2nd strongest WL underdrive setting (read assist)
111: Strongest WL underdrive setting (read assist)

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_l2tag_wl_bias_step_en_fuse
registers.description:
This fuse enables the stepped wordline feature. Stepped wordline enabled set to 1. Stepped wordline disabled set to 0.
The stepped wordline bias enable overrides the wordline bias setting in the 2nd and 3rd phase (if available) of the wordline if the stepped wordline is enabled.
The 1st phase will still use the normal wordline bias enable. If the stepped wordline bias is disabled, then the normal wordline bias setting will be applied to all phases of the wordline.

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_l2tag_wr_ast_en_fuse
registers.description: This is the disable write assist fuse setting for l2tagsubm/l2statesubm. Write assist enabled set to 1. Write assist disabled set to 0

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_l2tag_wr_ast_pw_fuse
registers.description:
This is the write assist pulse width setting fuse for l2tagsubm/l2statesubm.
(Bits 3 to 2 are nocon)
XX00: Short self-timed pulse for write assist
XX01: Long self-timed pulse for write assist
XX1X: Synchronous self-timed pulse for write assist

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_l2tag_wr_ast_strength_fuse
registers.description:
This fuse is used for write assist P clamp settings.
(Bit 2)
0: disable coupling(kick) for gate cap
1: enable coupling(kick) for gate cap
(Bit 1)
0: disable coupling for large wire cap
1: enable coupling for large wire cap
(Bit 0)
0: disable coupling for medium wire cap
1: enable coupling for medium wire cap

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_lcdyn_fuse
registers.description: boot value for lcdyn in wp3a. this is only 2 bits, so 2'b11 is appended at msb in rtl

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_ldo_vccana_sel1p25_fuse
registers.description: Select source of 'LDO_VCCANA_SEL1P25'

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_lfm_ratio_fuse
registers.description: LFM frequency for HWP DOMA logic in 100Mhz encoding

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_mclk_boot_ratio_fuse
registers.description: mod workpoint boot ratio; Same encoding as mod workpoint. Default 16.67Mhz encoding. Maybe different encoding if mclk_use_raw defeature bit is set in pma

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_ms_mchard_sig_en_fuse
registers.description: Enable the MCHARD signaling of MS MCAs out of HW reset. Becomes the reset value of MS_CR_DEBUG_DEFEATURE[MCHARD_SIGNAL_INDICATION_EN]

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_pll_mode_fuse
registers.description: PLL/FLL Mode selection fuse.

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_reset_throttle_fuse
registers.description: 0x2 encoding is the same as 0x3

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_scdyn_fuse
registers.description: boot value for scdyn in wp3a. this is only 2 bits, so 2'b11 is appended at msb in rtl

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_soc_type_fuse
registers.description:
Specifies whether the SOC is server or Client. 0=server, 1=client
      Certain PM flow handshakes such as PM_REQ and PM_COMMAND will be treated differently to be compatible with client vs server flow differences. Also PHASE_MASK behavior is different.

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_sram_highv_threshold_fuse
registers.description: sram_highv_threshold_vid is 11 bit threshold fuse. This is a value is milivolt/volts meaning the number programmed in this fuse will be multiplied by 2.5 mv to come up with a voltage value for the fuse. For example value 11'h000 is 0mv, 11'h001 is 2.5 mV, 11'h104 is 650mV. Programmed to the voltage when write assist (e.g. NBL) is turned off. Any voltage higher will turn off write assist. Reset default is 0x0, Note: this fuse has to be programmed such that the value is greater than FB_PMA_L2_VOLTAGE_THRESHOLD_FUSE. if the value is programmed as 7ff then this feature is disabled. Shared ownership with araman1

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_timer_corepg_rmp_fuse
registers.description: This fuse defines the cores pwrgate ramp step size

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_timer_delays_biu_clkon_fuse
registers.description: Delay to allow biu gnsclk enable propagation

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_timer_delays_biu_fw_on_fuse
registers.description: Delay to allow Biu/L2 firewall assertion toggle to propagate

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_timer_delays_biu_pg_off_fuse
registers.description: Delay to allow BIU Power Gate  powerdown to complete. In PGRAMP mode, this fuse should be set to '1 to minimize the delay

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_timer_delays_biu_rst_off_fuse
registers.description: Delay to allow BIU powergood assertion to propagate

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_timer_delays_biu_rst_on_fuse
registers.description: Delay to allow BIU reset assertion to propagate

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_timer_delays_biufw_off_fuse
registers.description: Delay to allow BIU firewall deassertion toggle to propagate

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_timer_delays_biupg_on_late_fuse
registers.description: Delay to allow BIU reset to propagate and initialize reset after power up.

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_timer_delays_core_clkon_fuse
registers.description: Delay to allow dfx_clock_enable, lcp_reset and clkcomp_reset signal state to propagate

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_timer_delays_core_fw_fuse
registers.description: Delay to allow Core firewall deassertion toggle to propagate

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_timer_delays_core_fw_on_fuse
registers.description: Delay to allow Core firewall assertion toggle to propagate

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_timer_delays_core_rst_fuse
registers.description: Core reset deassertion prop delay including propagation through reset HFN stages within the core... allows for up to 32 cycles at 100MHz worst case

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_timer_delays_core_rst_on_fuse
registers.description: Delay to allow Core reset assertion to propagate

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_timer_delays_corepg_on_fuse
registers.description: Delay to allow Core Power Gate Early ramp to complete when PGRAMP mode is disabled

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_timer_delays_corepg_on_late_fuse
registers.description: Delay to allow Core Power Gate Early ramp to complete when PGRAMP mode is disabled. This delay is also used to allow the core reset state to propagate on cryclk bypass and initialize the logic after power up

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_timer_delays_l2_pg_off_fuse
registers.description: Delay to allow L2 Power Gate  powerdown to complete. In PGRAMP mode, this fuse should be set to '1 to minimize the delay

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_timer_delays_l2pg_on_fuse
registers.description: Delay to allow L2 Power Gate Early ramp to complete when PGRAMP mode is disabled

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_timer_delays_l2pg_on_late_fuse
registers.description: Delay to allow L2 Power Gate Late ramp to complete. This delay is also used to allow biu and l2 reset state to propagate on cryclk bypass and initialize the logic after power up

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_timer_delays_ldo_transition_fuse
registers.description: Delay to wait for FIVR/DLVR to assert vr_settled

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_timer_delays_mod_cst_done_fuse
registers.description: Delay to allow BIU reset deassertion to propagate

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_timer_delays_mod_cst_exit_start_fuse
registers.description: Delay to allow lcp_reset and clkcomp_reset signal state to propagate

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_timer_delays_rosc_biu_on_fuse
registers.description: Delay to allow BIU clock to be switched to Ring Oscillator or Crystal Bypass

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_timer_delays_rosc_core_on_fuse
registers.description: Delay to allow Core clock to be switched to Ring Oscillator or Crystal Bypass

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_timer_delays_start_pll_lock_fuse
registers.description: CLPMA Timer Delay Fuse. START_PLL_LOCK

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_timer_delays_start_pll_prelock_fuse
registers.description: CLPMA Timer Delay Fuse. START_PLL_PRELOCK

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_timer_delays_stop_gns_fuse
registers.description: Delay to allow gnsclk disable to propagate

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_timer_delays_stop_wait_fuse
registers.description: Wait for pll_lock to deassert after disabling pll

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_timer_l2pg_rmp_fuse
registers.description: This fuse defines the L2 pwrgate ramp step size

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_u2c_data_cma_credits_fuse
registers.description: Configure the number of U2C DATA CMA entries/Credits. 0-credits not supported for stall mode

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_u2c_req_bgf_reduc1_fuse
registers.description: Configure the number of U2C REQ  BGF entries/Credits. 1-credits not supported for stall mode

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_u2c_rsp_cma_credits_fuse
registers.description: Configure the number of U2C RSP CMA entries/Credits. 0-credits not supported for stall mode

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pml_enable_fuse
registers.description: This fuse is used to control (1=enable, 0=disable) the EPT PML feature.

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pppe_enable_fuse
registers.description: This fuse controls (1=enable, 0=disable) Pronghorn Peak Platform Enhancements(PPPE) aka eXtended uCode (XuCode) feature

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pqc_enable_fuse
registers.description: Spare fuse now. Will be removed at the earlier convenience

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_prm_small_2m_fuse
registers.description: when set to 1, CPU supports 2M PRM size. Will be enabled for RYF Hetero C6 DRAM support (2MC, 2MEE). PRM_SMALL_* fuses are unique i.e. does not require to set together.

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_prm_small_4m_fuse
registers.description: when set to 1, CPU supports 4M PRM size. Will be enabled for FuSA Periodic Scan. PRM_SMALL_* fuses are unique i.e. does not require to set together.

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_prm_small_8m_fuse
registers.description: when set to 1, CPU supports 8M PRM size. Will be enabled for FuSA Periodic Scan. PRM_SMALL_* fuses are unique i.e. does not require to set together.

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_prmrr_enable_fuse
registers.description: This fuse is used to enable PRMRR MSR support as well as qualifying related flows

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_rao_ni_disable_fuse
registers.description: FeatureFuse to disable Remote Atomic Operations (RAO) new instructions

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_rar_disable_fuse
registers.description: This fuse is used to control (1=disable, 0=enable) the RAR feature.

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_rdwrmsrlist_wrmsrns_enable_fuse
registers.description: Enable RDMSRLIST, WRMSRLIST, WRMSRNS instructions.

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_rpc_enable_fuse
registers.description: SKT 14014938668: RDT Resource Priority Control

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_s3m_att_disable_fuse
registers.description: S3M Attestation Disable Fuse. Set for DICE based attestation

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_se_disable_fuse
registers.description: This fuse is used to control (0=enable, 1=disable) Secure Enclave(SE) aka Software Guard eXtension (SGX) feature  - VMCS PLA Color 6

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_seam_disable_fuse
registers.description: Disable TDX-SEAM (Secure Arbitration Mode)

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_smm_cpu_svrstr_disable_fuse

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_spp_dis_fuse

registers.name: fw_fuses_cluster0_atom_itd_slope
registers.description:
This parameter is used to adjust the slope for correcting the voltage ITD controlled domain at low temperatures
            The final 1/'C slope correction is proportional to the deltaV from the CUTOFF_V and deltaT from the CUTOFF_TJ.

registers.name: fw_fuses_cluster0_atom_itd_slope2
registers.description:

            The final 1/'C slope correction is proportional to the deltaV from the CUTOFF_V and deltaT from the CUTOFF_TJ.

registers.description: The voltage from which and below, ITD effect is relevant. The fuse is in U9.1.8 volts

registers.name: fw_fuses_cluster1_atom_leak_params_eq_vt
registers.description:
Leakage equation correction VT.
                         Correction for the leakage temperature multiplier in the equation e^(aV+bT-cVT)
                         Important note: It is burnt as positive number and Pcode is subtracting.

registers.name: core_fuse_core_fuse_acode_core_ia_p0_ratio_avx256
registers.description:
P0 when running AVX256 instructions. This is an absolute ration, not delta.
              That is, any core that runs AVX-256 will not run faster.

registers.name: core_fuse_core_fuse_acode_ia_itd_slope
registers.description:
This parameter is used to adjust the slope for correcting the voltage ITD controlled domain at low temperatures
              The final 1/'C slope correction is proportional to the deltaV from the CUTOFF_V and deltaT from the CUTOFF_TJ.
              The fuse is in U9.-7.16 (1/C).

registers.name: core_fuse_core_fuse_acode_ia_itd_slope

This parameter is used to adjust the slope for correcting the voltage ITD controlled domain at low temperatures
              The final 1/'C slope correction is proportional to the deltaV from the CUTOFF_V and deltaT from the CUTOFF_TJ.
              The fuse is in U9.-7.16 (1/C).

registers.name: core_fuse_core_fuse_acode_ia_leak
registers.description:
Leakage current in U11.5.6 amperes for a nominal IA core at T=Tref and V=Vref, including AVX. Expected range: Above zero.
              On top of this, we have per-core factor driven by IA_LEAK_VAR fuses. Each core has it's own factor.
              And the leakage equations.This is a Mulitiplication of the following Pcode fuses FUSES_IA_LEAK*FUSES_CORE_IA_LEAK_VAR_RANGE - Check for typical/min/max values

registers.name: core_fuse_core_fuse_acode_ia_leak
registers.description:
Leakage current in U11.5.6 amperes for a nominal IA core at T=Tref and V=Vref, including AVX. Expected range: Above zero.
              On top of this, we have per-core factor driven by IA_LEAK_VAR fuses. Each core has it's own factor.
              And the leakage equations.This is a Mulitiplication of the following Pcode fuses FUSES_IA_LEAK*FUSES_CORE_IA_LEAK_VAR_RANGE - Check for typical/min/max values

registers.name: core_fuse_core_fuse_acode_ia_leak_eq_t
registers.description:
Leakage equation correction T.
              Correction for the leakage temperature multiplier in the equation e^(aV+bT+cVT) - Check for typical/min/max values

registers.name: core_fuse_core_fuse_acode_ia_leak_eq_t
registers.description:
Leakage equation correction T.
              Correction for the leakage temperature multiplier in the equation e^(aV+bT+cVT) - Check for typical/min/max values

registers.name: core_fuse_core_fuse_acode_ia_leak_eq_v
registers.description:
Leakage equation correction V.
              Correction for the leakage temperature multiplier in the equation e^(aV+bT+cVT)  - Check for typical/min/max values

registers.name: core_fuse_core_fuse_acode_ia_leak_eq_v
registers.description:
Leakage equation correction V.

registers.name: core_fuse_core_fuse_misc_physical_address_size
registers.description:
Physical address size supported in the core low two bits (Assuming uncore is 44 by default). Values not defined in the table are reserved.
# SNC: updated encodings as it was new encodings were required
#
#  Mode   |    Mask     | fuse encoding
# --------|-------------|--------------
# 32 bits | 0xffff_ffff |    '0001 -- GLM Reserved - Do not use
# 36 bits | 0xffff_fff0 |    '0011
# 39 bits | 0xffff_ff80 |    '0000
# 40 bits | 0xffff_ff00 |    '0101
# 44 bits | 0xffff_f000 |    '0111
# 46 bits | 0xffff_c000 |    '1000
# 50 bits | 0xfffc_0000 |    '1010
# 52 bits | 0xfff0_0000 |    '1011

registers.description:
IMPORTANT:
FUSE ALWAYS SET, NOT IN USE, wil be removed in future.
When set, support of x2APIC (Extended APIC) in the core and unCore is enabled.  The fuse being set will impact :
a. CPUID indication x2APIC support
b. UCODE ability to enable x2APIC support
c. Uncore ability to generate and send x2APIC messages.

registers.description: AFC GV frequency ramp rate in approximate GHz/us slope. Encoding: 0x0 = unconstrained, 0x1 = 0.25, 0x2 = 0.33, 0x3 = 0.45, 0x4 = 0.60, 0x5 = 0.75, 0x6 = 0.90, 0x7 = 1.1, 0x8 = 1.4, 0x9 = 1.8, 0xA = 2.3, 0xB = 2.9, 0xC = 3.6, 0xD = 4.7, 0xE = 6.6, 0xF = 9.4

registers.name: core_fuse_pll_fuse_fusecr_fll_perf_0_raw_lock_thresh
registers.description: Frequency based lock detector threshold defined as allowed percentage error of target frequency count. Encoding: 0x0 = 0.75%, 0x1 = 1.5%, 0x2 = 3.0%, 0x3 = 6.0%.

registers.description: FLL DCOA IREF strength. Encoding: 0x0 = minimum strength, 0xF = maximum strength.

registers.name: core_fuse_pll_fuse_fusecr_fll_proc_0_dco_settle_time

registers.name: core_fuse_pll_fuse_fusecr_fll_proc_0_dco_settle_time

registers.name: core_fuse_pll_fuse_fusecr_fll_proc_0_gv_target_offset

registers.name: fw_fuses_cluster0_bigcore_itd_slope
registers.description:
This parameter is used to adjust the slope for correcting the voltage ITD controlled domain at low temperatures
            The final 1/'C slope correction is proportional to the deltaV from the CUTOFF_V and deltaT from the CUTOFF_TJ.

registers.name: fw_fuses_cluster0_bigcore_itd_slope2
registers.description:
This parameter is used to adjust the slope for correcting the voltage ITD controlled domain at low temperatures
            The final 1/'C slope correction is proportional to the deltaV from the CUTOFF_V and deltaT from the CUTOFF_TJ.

registers.name: fw_fuses_cluster0_bigcore_leak_params_eq_v
registers.description:
Leakage equation correction V.
                         Correction for the leakage temperature multiplier in the equation e^(aV+bT-cVT)

registers.name: fw_fuses_cluster0_bigcore_leak_params_eq_vt
registers.description:
Leakage equation correction VT.
                         Correction for the leakage temperature multiplier in the equation e^(aV+bT-cVT)
                         Important note: It is burnt as positive number and Pcode is subtracting.

registers.name: fw_fuses_cluster0_ccp_0_ia_cdyn_var
registers.description:
Correction factor on top of IA_CDYN_MAX fuse, for this specific core.
                         This core Cdyn = IA_CDYN_MAX * this_fuse.
                         Values supported are between 0.0 and almost 2.0 of the nominal value.

registers.name: fw_fuses_cluster0_ccp_0_ia_leak_var
registers.description:
Correction factor on top of IA_LEAK fuse, for this specific core.
                         This core Leak = IA_LEAK * this_fuse.
                         Values supported are between 0.0 and almost 2.0 of the nominal value.

registers.name: fw_fuses_cluster0_ccp_0_ia_p0_ratio_downbin
registers.description:
Determines max ratio for this core by subtracting this value from IA_P0_RATIO.
                This is part of Favored Core Turbo (FCT) feature.
                As this is Turbo feature, the result P0 (IA_P0_RATIO - this fuse) must still be at least P1 (IA_P1_RATIO)

registers.name: fw_fuses_cluster0_ccp_1_ia_cdyn_var
registers.description:
Correction factor on top of IA_CDYN_MAX fuse, for this specific core.
                         This core Cdyn = IA_CDYN_MAX * this_fuse.
                         Values supported are between 0.0 and almost 2.0 of the nominal value.

registers.name: fw_fuses_cluster0_ccp_1_ia_leak_var
registers.description:
Correction factor on top of IA_LEAK fuse, for this specific core.
                         This core Leak = IA_LEAK * this_fuse.
                         Values supported are between 0.0 and almost 2.0 of the nominal value.

registers.name: fw_fuses_cluster1_ccp_ia_leak_var
registers.description:
Correction factor on top of IA_LEAK fuse, for this specific core.
                         This core Leak = IA_LEAK * this_fuse.
                         Values supported are between 0.0 and almost 2.0 of the nominal value.

registers.name: fw_fuses_cluster1_ccp_ia_p0_ratio_downbin
registers.description:
Determines max ratio for this core by subtracting this value from IA_P0_RATIO.
                This is part of Favored Core Turbo (FCT) feature.
                As this is Turbo feature, the result P0 (IA_P0_RATIO - this fuse) must still be at least P1 (IA_P1_RATIO)

registers.name: fw_fuses_config_tdp_ratio_1
registers.description: This ratio is used as the guaranteed ratio (in place of IA_P1_RATIO) whenever Config TDP level 1 is active. A value of 0 disables this configurable TDP point. If both points are disabled then Config TDP is disabled.

registers.name: fw_fuses_config_tdp_ratio_2
registers.description:
This ratio is used as the guaranteed ratio (in place of IA_P1_RATIO) whenever Config TDP level 2 is active. A value of 0 disables this configurable TDP point. If both points are disabled then Config TDP is disabled.
            Note: Level 2 can be non-zero only if level 1 is.

registers.name: fw_fuses_dlvr_media_vout_to_vin_b_coeff
registers.description: Coefficient B of Vin calculation from Vout over the DLVR, according to the formula: Vin = Vout + B*Vout + C + TEMP(Tref-Tmin)/Tref

registers.name: fw_fuses_dlvr_media_vout_to_vin_temp_coeff
registers.description: Coefficient TEMP of Vin calculation from Vout over the DLVR, according to the formula: Vin = Vout + B*Vout + C + TEMP(Tref-Tmin)/Tref

registers.name: fw_fuses_ia_cdyn_max
registers.description:
Maximal dynamic capacitance for a nominal IA core at Vref in U10.5.5(nF), for IccProtector=0, i.e., 128-bit light (non AVX).
                         On top of this, we have per-core factor driven by IA_CDYN_VAR fuses. Each core has it's own factor.
                         And IA_CDYN_V_CORRECTION factor.
                         And on top of this, we have AVX correction factores, hard-coded in pcode, not fusing.

registers.name: fw_fuses_ia_cdyn_v_correction
registers.description: Correction of the Cdyn in the voltage. Cdyn(V)=CdynRef*(1+CdynDv*(V-Vref))

registers.name: fw_fuses_ia_cep_corrected_vref_m1
registers.description: This fuse is used for CEP threshold encoding, used to correct non-linearity of DAC. Range of up to 1.5V

registers.name: fw_fuses_ia_cep_enable
registers.description: This fuse determines if the IA CEP is enabled

registers.name: fw_fuses_ia_cep_temperature_coefficient
registers.description: This fuse is used for CEP threshold encoding, used to correct temperature dependency of DAC.

registers.name: fw_fuses_ia_cep_voltage_offset
registers.description: This fuse is used for CEP threshold encoding, used to correct non-linearity of DAC. Range of -0.5V to 0.5V

registers.name: fw_fuses_ia_leak
registers.description:
Leakage current in U11.5.6 amperes for a nominal IA core or full Atom module at T=Tref and V=Vref, including AVX. Expected range: Above zero.
                         On top of this, we have per-core factor driven by IA_LEAK_VAR fuses. Each core has it's own factor.
                         And the leakage equations.

registers.name: fw_fuses_ia_min_ratio
registers.description: Minimum ratio for IA domain in 100Mhz. Normal values are 4, 5, 6, 7, or 8. 6bits as used for enforce fixed freq by DFx flows

registers.name: fw_fuses_ia_p0_ratio
registers.description: max turbo point in 100 MHz- MAX_TURBO_RATIO

registers.name: fw_fuses_ia_p0_ratio_1_atom_corenum
registers.description:
Defines the threshold in terms of number of active cores at which the maximum turbo ratio will be reduced by corresponding IA_P0_RATIO_*_ATOM_DELTA bins.
                          0 value will be ignored by Pcode. Last populated (non 0) IA_P0_RATIO_x_ATOM_CORENUM should be set to maximum number of Atom cores.
                          Fuses must be populated in ascending order, i.e. IA_P0_RATIO_[i]_ATOM_CORENUM is greater than IA_P0_RATIO_[i-1]_ATOM_CORENUM.

registers.name: fw_fuses_ia_p0_ratio_1_atom_delta
registers.description: lower Atom Core frequency of the 1st ATOM_CORENUM group. core frequency=P(0) frequency - 1_atom_delta

registers.name: fw_fuses_ia_p0_ratio_2_atom_corenum
registers.description:
Defines the threshold in terms of number of active cores at which the maximum turbo ratio will be reduced by corresponding IA_P0_RATIO_*_ATOM_DELTA bins.
                          0 value will be ignored by Pcode. Last populated (non 0) IA_P0_RATIO_x_ATOM_CORENUM should be set to maximum number of Atom cores.
                          Fuses must be populated in ascending order, i.e. IA_P0_RATIO_[i]_ATOM_CORENUM is greater than IA_P0_RATIO_[i-1]_ATOM_CORENUM.

registers.name: fw_fuses_ia_p0_ratio_2_atom_delta
registers.description: lower Atom Core frequency of the 2nd ATOM_CORENUM group. core frequency=P(0) frequency - 1_atom_delta - 2_atom_delta

registers.name: fw_fuses_ia_p0_ratio_2_bigcore_delta
registers.description: lower Big IA frequency due to 2 active Big IA cores. core frequency=P(0) frequency - 2_bigcore_delta

registers.name: fw_fuses_ia_p0_ratio_3_atom_corenum
registers.description:
Defines the threshold in terms of number of active cores at which the maximum turbo ratio will be reduced by corresponding IA_P0_RATIO_*_ATOM_DELTA bins.
                          0 value will be ignored by Pcode. Last populated (non 0) IA_P0_RATIO_x_ATOM_CORENUM should be set to maximum number of Atom cores.
                          Fuses must be populated in ascending order, i.e. IA_P0_RATIO_[i]_ATOM_CORENUM is greater than IA_P0_RATIO_[i-1]_ATOM_CORENUM.

registers.name: fw_fuses_ia_p0_ratio_3_atom_delta
registers.description: lower Atom Core frequency of the 3rd ATOM_CORENUM group. core frequency=P(0) frequency - 1_atom_delta - ... - 3_atom_delta

registers.name: fw_fuses_ia_p0_ratio_3_bigcore_delta
registers.description: lower Big IA frequency due to 3 active Big IA cores. core frequency=P(0) frequency - 2_bigcore_delta - 3_bigcore_delta

registers.name: fw_fuses_ia_p0_ratio_4_atom_corenum

                          0 value will be ignored by Pcode. Last populated (non 0) IA_P0_RATIO_x_ATOM_CORENUM should be set to maximum number of Atom cores.
                          Fuses must be populated in ascending order, i.e. IA_P0_RATIO_[i]_ATOM_CORENUM is greater than IA_P0_RATIO_[i-1]_ATOM_CORENUM.

registers.name: fw_fuses_ia_p0_ratio_4_atom_delta
registers.description: lower Atom Core frequency of the 4th ATOM_CORENUM group. core frequency=P(0) frequency - 1_atom_delta - ... - 4_atom_delta

registers.name: fw_fuses_ia_p0_ratio_4_bigcore_delta
registers.description: lower Big IA frequency due to 4 active Big IA cores. core frequency=P(0) frequency - 2_bigcore_delta - ... - 4_bigcore_delta

registers.name: fw_fuses_ia_p0_ratio_5_atom_corenum
registers.description:
Defines the threshold in terms of number of active cores at which the maximum turbo ratio will be reduced by corresponding IA_P0_RATIO_*_ATOM_DELTA bins.
                          0 value will be ignored by Pcode. Last populated (non 0) IA_P0_RATIO_x_ATOM_CORENUM should be set to maximum number of Atom cores.
                          Fuses must be populated in ascending order, i.e. IA_P0_RATIO_[i]_ATOM_CORENUM is greater than IA_P0_RATIO_[i-1]_ATOM_CORENUM.

registers.name: fw_fuses_ia_p0_ratio_5_atom_delta
registers.description: lower Atom Core frequency of the 5th ATOM_CORENUM group. core frequency=P(0) frequency - 1_atom_delta - ... - 5_atom_delta

registers.name: fw_fuses_ia_p0_ratio_5_bigcore_delta
registers.description: lower Big IA frequency due to 5 active Big IA cores. core frequency=P(0) frequency - 2_bigcore_delta - ...- 5_bigcore_delta

registers.name: fw_fuses_ia_p0_ratio_6_atom_corenum
registers.description:
Defines the threshold in terms of number of active cores at which the maximum turbo ratio will be reduced by corresponding IA_P0_RATIO_*_ATOM_DELTA bins.
                          0 value will be ignored by Pcode. Last populated (non 0) IA_P0_RATIO_x_ATOM_CORENUM should be set to maximum number of Atom cores.
                          Fuses must be populated in ascending order, i.e. IA_P0_RATIO_[i]_ATOM_CORENUM is greater than IA_P0_RATIO_[i-1]_ATOM_CORENUM.

registers.name: fw_fuses_ia_p0_ratio_6_atom_delta
registers.description: lower Atom Core frequency of the 6th ATOM_CORENUM group. core frequency=P(0) frequency - 1_atom_delta - ... - 6_atom_delta

registers.name: fw_fuses_ia_p0_ratio_6_bigcore_delta
registers.description: lower Big IA frequency due to 6 active Big IA cores. core frequency=P(0) frequency - 2_bigcore_delta - ...- 6_bigcore_delta

registers.name: fw_fuses_ia_p0_ratio_7_atom_corenum
registers.description:
Defines the threshold in terms of number of active cores at which the maximum turbo ratio will be reduced by corresponding IA_P0_RATIO_*_ATOM_DELTA bins.
                          0 value will be ignored by Pcode. Last populated (non 0) IA_P0_RATIO_x_ATOM_CORENUM should be set to maximum number of Atom cores.
                          Fuses must be populated in ascending order, i.e. IA_P0_RATIO_[i]_ATOM_CORENUM is greater than IA_P0_RATIO_[i-1]_ATOM_CORENUM.

registers.name: fw_fuses_ia_p0_ratio_7_atom_delta
registers.description: lower Atom Core frequency of the 7th ATOM_CORENUM group. core frequency=P(0) frequency - 1_atom_delta - ... - 7_atom_delta

registers.name: fw_fuses_ia_p0_ratio_7_bigcore_delta
registers.description: lower Big IA frequency due to 7 active Big IA cores. core frequency=P(0) frequency - 2_bigcore_delta - ...- 7_bigcore_delta

registers.name: fw_fuses_ia_p0_ratio_8_atom_corenum
registers.description:
Defines the threshold in terms of number of active cores at which the maximum turbo ratio will be reduced by corresponding IA_P0_RATIO_*_ATOM_DELTA bins.
                          0 value will be ignored by Pcode. Last populated (non 0) IA_P0_RATIO_x_ATOM_CORENUM should be set to maximum number of Atom cores.
                          Fuses must be populated in ascending order, i.e. IA_P0_RATIO_[i]_ATOM_CORENUM is greater than IA_P0_RATIO_[i-1]_ATOM_CORENUM.

registers.description: lower Atom Core frequency of the 8th ATOM_CORENUM group. core frequency=P(0) frequency - 1_atom_delta - ... - 8_atom_delta

registers.name: fw_fuses_ia_p0_ratio_avx256_delta
registers.description:
Amount to lower P0 when running AVX256 instructions.
                         That is, any core that runs AVX-256 will not run faster than: IA_P0_RATIO - IA_P0_RATIO_AVX256_DELTA.
                         Setting this fuse will force the lower frequency even if there is no IccMax or power limitations, so use with care.

registers.name: fw_fuses_ia_p1_ratio
registers.description: guaranteed point frequency in 100 MHz - Max_Non_Turbo_Ratio

registers.name: fw_fuses_ia_pn_ratio
registers.description: most efficient point frequency in 100 MHz - Max_Efficiency_Ratio

registers.name: fw_fuses_ia_svid_vr_offset_vid
registers.description:
This fuse determines the VID offset of the IA SVID VR. value 0 means disable. MSB is there just to enable
            writing zero, otherwise it is ignored. 7 LSBs are in VID code for IMVP8. The feature needs to be enabled by STRAP
            before the VID offset takes effect. This definition is copied from CFL.

registers.name: fw_fuses_sa_dlvr_media_supported
registers.description:
Allows for specific DLVR to enter in regulation. (1= enable, 0 = disable).
              If this OR result is 0 (disable), the DLVR will be in constant bypass mode (never regulated, might be off).
              Default is 0 (disabled).

registers.name: fw_fuses_gt_acm_enable
registers.description: This fuse determines if the GT ACM is enabled

registers.name: fw_fuses_gt_acm_jagr_0
registers.description: Fuse of ACM_JAGR parameter per frequency = Vagr / Vpg

registers.name: fw_fuses_gt_acm_jagr_1
registers.description: Fuse of ACM_JAGR parameter per frequency = Vagr / Vpg

registers.name: fw_fuses_gt_acm_jagr_2
registers.description: Fuse of ACM_JAGR parameter per frequency = Vagr / Vpg

registers.name: fw_fuses_gt_acm_jagr_3
registers.description: Fuse of ACM_JAGR parameter per frequency = Vagr / Vpg

registers.name: fw_fuses_gt_acm_jagr_4
registers.description: Fuse of ACM_JAGR parameter per frequency = Vagr / Vpg

registers.name: fw_fuses_gt_acm_jagr_5
registers.description: Fuse of ACM_JAGR parameter per frequency = Vagr / Vpg

registers.name: fw_fuses_gt_cdyn_max
registers.description: Maximal dynamic capacitance for GT, at Vref in U11.6.5(nF).

registers.name: fw_fuses_gt_cdyn_v_correction
registers.description: Correction of the Cdyn in the voltage. Cdyn(V)=CdynRef*(1+CdynDv*(V-Vref))

registers.name: fw_fuses_gt_ebb_trim_voltage_level_0
registers.description: voltage point for the selecting eeb_dynamictrim group

registers.name: fw_fuses_gt_ebb_trim_voltage_level_1
registers.description: voltage point for the selecting eeb_dynamictrim group

registers.name: fw_fuses_gt_ebb_trim_voltage_level_2
registers.description: voltage point for the selecting eeb_dynamictrim group

registers.name: fw_fuses_gt_ebb_trim_voltage_level_3
registers.description: voltage point for the selecting eeb_dynamictrim group

registers.name: fw_fuses_gt_ebb_trim_voltage_level_4
registers.description: voltage point for the selecting eeb_dynamictrim group

registers.name: fw_fuses_gt_itd_cutoff_v
registers.description: The voltage from which and below, ITD effect is relevant. The fuse is in U9.1.8 volts

registers.name: fw_fuses_gt_itd_cutoff_v2
registers.description: The voltage from which and below, ITD effect is relevant. The fuse is in U9.1.8 volts

registers.name: fw_fuses_gt_itd_slope
registers.description:
This parameter is used to adjust the slope for correcting the voltage ITD controlled domain at low temperatures
            The final 1/'C slope correction is proportional to the deltaV from the CUTOFF_V and deltaT from the CUTOFF_TJ.

registers.name: fw_fuses_gt_itd_slope2
registers.description:
This parameter is used to adjust the slope for correcting the voltage ITD controlled domain at low temperatures
            The final 1/'C slope correction is proportional to the deltaV from the CUTOFF_V and deltaT from the CUTOFF_TJ.

registers.name: fw_fuses_gt_l3_sleep_disable
registers.description: Disable GT L3 Sleep functionality.

registers.name: fw_fuses_gt_l3_sleep_v_thr
registers.description: GT L3 Sleep Voltage Threshold in U0.7Volts. Below this voltage, sleep should be disabled

registers.name: fw_fuses_gt_leak_max
registers.description: Leakage current in U12.6.6 amperes for GT, at T=Tref and V=Vref.

registers.name: fw_fuses_gt_leak_params_eq_t
registers.description:
Leakage equation correction T.
                         Correction for the leakage temperature multiplier in the equation e^(aV+bT-cVT)

registers.name: fw_fuses_gt_leak_params_eq_v
registers.description:
Leakage equation correction V.
                         Correction for the leakage temperature multiplier in the equation e^(aV+bT-cVT)

registers.name: fw_fuses_gt_leak_params_eq_vt
registers.description:
Leakage equation correction VT.
                         Correction for the leakage temperature multiplier in the equation e^(aV+bT-cVT)
                         Important note: It is burnt as positive number and Pcode is subtracting.

registers.name: fw_fuses_gt_min_ratio
registers.description: Minimum ratio for GT domain in 50 Mhz. Normal Value is 2. 6bits as used for enforce fixed freq by DFx flows

registers.name: fw_fuses_gt_p0_ratio
registers.description:
GT maximal frequency, the GT Unslice P0 ratio, in 50 MHz bins.
                         The P0 for GT Slices is GT_P0_RATIO - GT_P0_RATIO_1SLICE_DELTA, or lower if more slices are active.
                         On GT2 products, it will also push the Unslice down, as GT has a single frequency.

registers.name: fw_fuses_gt_p1_ratio
registers.description:
Guaranteed non-turbo frequency in 50Mhz.
                         This is an HVM fused value used by pcode and reflected to the driver.

registers.name: fw_fuses_gt_pn_ratio
registers.description:
Lowest requested p-state can be equal or greater than the real minimum in 50 MHz.
                          This is HVM fused value used by pcode and reflected to the driver

registers.name: fw_fuses_gt_rf_aging_disable
registers.description: Disable GT RF aging control.

registers.name: fw_fuses_gt_rf_aging_v_threshold
registers.description: GT RF aging Threshold in U1.7Volts. Below this voltage, aging control should be disabled

registers.name: fw_fuses_gt_vf_ratio_1

registers.name: dlvr_media_fuse_awa_snapshot_delay
registers.description: number of cycles to wait between awa trigger to snapshot of the 8 awa regs

registers.name: dlvr_media_fuse_bldr_en
registers.description: activate bleeder during ramp to target or in DFT mode

registers.name: dlvr_media_fuse_bldr_leg
registers.description: Controls bleeder strength and the bias current for bleeder, default 100 = up to 1A

registers.name: dlvr_media_fuse_cmb_disable
registers.description: disable aip combo protection block (bit2 - corruption event, bit1 - dynamic PGmode, bit0 - droop mitigation)

registers.name: dlvr_media_fuse_cmb_thrshbot
registers.description: voltage threshold for droop need to add to DLVR WorkPoint in mV

registers.name: dlvr_media_fuse_cmb_thrshtop
registers.description: voltage threshold for overshoot need to add to DLVR WorkPoint in mV

registers.name: dlvr_media_fuse_cmb_trimofst0
registers.description: offset trimming for comparators

registers.name: dlvr_media_fuse_cmb_trimofst1
registers.description: offset trimming for comparators

registers.name: dlvr_media_fuse_cps_alpha_coeff
registers.description: CPS alpha coefficient U1.11, default 1.431

registers.name: dlvr_media_fuse_cps_beta_coeff
registers.description: CPS Beta coefficient U1.11, default 1.326

registers.name: dlvr_media_fuse_cpsoffst
registers.description: add delay to CPS sublsb, lsb and msb deassertion to fix break before make PG open issue

registers.name: dlvr_media_fuse_dac_offset
registers.description: dac offset , default is 0.5 dac_code for round

registers.name: dlvr_media_fuse_dac_slope

registers.name: dlvr_media_fuse_daccode_minus_delta
registers.description: DAC minus delta - default 150mV  - each code is 4.19mV

registers.name: dlvr_media_fuse_daccode_plus_delta
registers.description: DAC plus delta - default 150mV - each code is 4.19mV

registers.name: dlvr_media_fuse_dlvr_dfspare10
registers.description: spare

registers.name: dlvr_media_fuse_down_ramprate_val
registers.description: down ramp rate value (units are 8 X 400 / fuse_val = mV/usec) , diffault is 520mV/usec

registers.name: dlvr_media_fuse_droopeventjump
registers.description: how many PG will be opened during droop event , default 011 - jump 40% for MaxLeg=11

registers.name: dlvr_media_fuse_go2pgcmp_cfg
registers.description: select block configuration (w/o filter, force 0/1 to output)

registers.name: dlvr_media_fuse_go2pgcmp_trimofst
registers.description: offset trimming for comparator

registers.name: dlvr_media_fuse_go2pgcmp_vindiv
registers.description: select voltage division of Vccin to the comparator input, default go2pg detect 7% HR

registers.name: dlvr_media_fuse_go2pgeventstep

registers.name: dlvr_media_fuse_intlvr_bsrtrim
registers.description: internal LVR rtrim for both DLVRs

registers.name: dlvr_media_fuse_ldoband_cfg
registers.description: low dropout cmp configuration : hysteresis, Dig Filter, Analog Filter

registers.name: dlvr_media_fuse_ldoband_trimofst

registers.name: dlvr_media_fuse_ldobandvindiv_sel

registers.name: dlvr_media_fuse_mxlg4lincmp_fltcfg

registers.name: dlvr_media_fuse_mxlg4lincmp_gain4range

registers.name: dlvr_media_fuse_mxlg4lincmp_hyst
registers.description: set hysteresis voltage for maxleg comparator

registers.name: dlvr_media_fuse_mxlg4lincmp_hystcfg

registers.name: dlvr_media_fuse_mxlg4lincmp_trimofst0

registers.name: dlvr_media_fuse_mxlg4lincmp_trimofst1
registers.description: offset trimming for comparator #1

registers.name: dlvr_media_fuse_mxlg4lincmp_trimofst2
registers.description: offset trimming for comparator #2

registers.name: dlvr_media_fuse_mxlg4lincmpvindiv_sel0
registers.description: select voltage division of Vccdlvrin to the comparator0 input

registers.name: dlvr_media_fuse_mxlg4lincmpvindiv_sel1
registers.description: select voltage division of Vccdlvrin to the comparator1 input

registers.name: dlvr_media_fuse_mxlg4lincmpvindiv_sel2

registers.name: fw_fuses_dlvr_ring_vout_to_vin_b_coeff
registers.description: Coefficient B of Vin calculation from Vout over the DLVR, according to the formula: Vin = Vout + B*Vout + C + TEMP(Tref-Tmin)/Tref

registers.name: fw_fuses_dlvr_ring_vout_to_vin_temp_coeff
registers.description: Coefficient TEMP of Vin calculation from Vout over the DLVR, according to the formula: Vin = Vout + B*Vout + C + TEMP(Tref-Tmin)/Tref

registers.name: fw_fuses_llc_nbl_threshold
registers.description:
Voltage threshold for NBL, above this voltage, NBL is disabled.
                          Value of zero means no dynamic NBL (NBL is always active).

registers.name: fw_fuses_llc_num_snoop_filter_ways
registers.description:
The additional ways for snoop filter, not including the Tag ways that are attached to data ways in LLC.
                Core needs to know the number of snoop filter ways as it is needed for WRMSR(SF_QOS_MASK_n) flow.
                Atom core uses this info also for INVD/WBINVD flow.
                Populated in PUNIT in PM_SOC_INFO_CARD. ADL HSD:1306306623.

registers.name: fw_fuses_ring_cdyn_max
registers.description: Maximal dynamic capacitance for Ring/LLC at Vref in U5.5(nF)

registers.name: fw_fuses_ring_cdyn_v_correction
registers.description: Correction of the Cdyn in the voltage. Cdyn(V)=CdynRef*(1+CdynDv*(V-Vref))

registers.name: fw_fuses_ring_dlvr_supported
registers.description:
Allows for specific DLVR to enter in regulation. (1= enable, 0 = disable).
              If this OR result is 0 (disable), the DLVR will be in constant bypass mode (never regulated, might be off).
              Default is 0 (disabled).

registers.name: fw_fuses_ring_itd_cutoff_v
registers.description: The voltage from which and below, ITD effect is relevant. The fuse is in U9.1.8 volts

registers.name: fw_fuses_ring_itd_cutoff_v2
registers.description: The voltage from which and below, ITD effect is relevant. The fuse is in U9.1.8 volts

registers.name: fw_fuses_ring_itd_slope
registers.description:
This parameter is used to adjust the slope for correcting the voltage ITD controlled domain at low temperatures
            The final 1/'C slope correction is proportional to the deltaV from the CUTOFF_V and deltaT from the CUTOFF_TJ.

registers.name: fw_fuses_ring_itd_slope2
registers.description:
This parameter is used to adjust the slope for correcting the voltage ITD controlled domain at low temperatures
            The final 1/'C slope correction is proportional to the deltaV from the CUTOFF_V and deltaT from the CUTOFF_TJ.

registers.name: fw_fuses_ring_leak
registers.description: Leakage current in U11.5.6 amperes for all LLC, CBOs and Ring at T=Tref and V=Vref

registers.name: fw_fuses_ring_leak_params_eq_t
registers.description:
Leakage equation correction T.
                         Correction for the leakage temperature multiplier in the equation e^(aV+bT-cVT)

registers.name: fw_fuses_ring_leak_params_eq_v
registers.description:
Leakage equation correction V.
                         Correction for the leakage temperature multiplier in the equation e^(aV+bT-cVT)

registers.name: fw_fuses_ring_leak_params_eq_vt
registers.description:
Leakage equation correction VT.
                         Correction for the leakage temperature multiplier in the equation e^(aV+bT-cVT)
                         Important note: It is burnt as positive number and Pcode is subtracting.

              If this OR result is 0 (disable), the DLVR will be in constant bypass mode (never regulated, might be off).
              Default is 0 (disabled).

registers.name: fw_fuses_sa_dlvr_vpu_supported
registers.description:
Allows for specific DLVR to enter in regulation. (1= enable, 0 = disable).
              If this OR result is 0 (disable), the DLVR will be in constant bypass mode (never regulated, might be off).
              Default is 0 (disabled)

registers.name: fw_fuses_sa_itd_cutoff_v

registers.name: fw_fuses_sa_itd_cutoff_v2
registers.description: The voltage from which and below, ITD effect is relevant. The fuse is in U9.1.8 volts

registers.name: fw_fuses_sa_itd_slope
registers.description:
This parameter is used to adjust the slope for correcting the voltage ITD controlled domain at low temperatures
            The final 1/'C slope correction is proportional to the deltaV from the CUTOFF_V and deltaT from the CUTOFF_TJ.

registers.name: fw_fuses_sa_itd_slope2
registers.description:
This parameter is used to adjust the slope for correcting the voltage ITD controlled domain at low temperatures
            The final 1/'C slope correction is proportional to the deltaV from the CUTOFF_V and deltaT from the CUTOFF_TJ.

registers.name: fw_fuses_sa_leak_params_eq_t
registers.description:
Leakage equation correction T.
                         Correction for the leakage temperature multiplier in the equation e^(aV+bT-cVT)

registers.name: fw_fuses_sa_leak_params_eq_v
registers.description:
Leakage equation correction V.
                         Correction for the leakage temperature multiplier in the equation e^(aV+bT-cVT)

registers.name: fw_fuses_sa_leak_params_eq_vt
registers.description:
Leakage equation correction VT.
                         Correction for the leakage temperature multiplier in the equation e^(aV+bT-cVT)
                         Important note: It is burnt as positive number and Pcode is subtracting.

registers.name: auxpll_fuse_fusecr_app_0_pll_gv_vid_boost_disable
registers.description: PLL mode FGV VID boost disable. Encoding: 0x0 = boost request enabled, 0x1 = boost request disabled.

registers.name: auxpll_fuse_fusecr_app_0_tllm_en
registers.description: Tight loop lock mode enable; 0x0 = Disable tight loop lock mode; 0x1 = enable tight loop lock mode

registers.name: auxpll_fuse_fusecr_app_0_tllm_output_gating
registers.description: Tight loop lock mode distribution clock gating control. Encoding: 0x0 = gate clock until final lock (tight-loop feedback and static DCC) or early lock (long-loop feedback or active DCC); 0x1 = gate clock until early lock; 0x2 = reserved; 0x3 = no clock gating during lock acquisition.

registers.name: auxpll_fuse_fusecr_dcc_0_dcs_offset_dca_static
registers.description: Duty cycle offset target with DCS enabled; static duty cycle setting with DCS enabled; 0x00=minimum high phase, 0x20=center setting, 0x3F=maximum high phase

registers.name: auxpll_fuse_fusecr_fusa_0_fll_lockthresh_postlock
registers.description: Frequency based FLL lock detector threshold for FuSa defined as allowed percentage error of target frequency count. Encoding: 0x0 = 0.75%, 0x1 = 1.5%, 0x2 = 3.0%, 0x3 = 6.0%.

registers.name: auxpll_fuse_fusecr_fusa_0_fusa_unlockerr_cfg
registers.description: FuSa unlock error indicator feature enable and reset condition: 0x0=disabled, 0x1=reset on side_rst, 0x2=reset on PLL disable, 0x3=reset on power-good de-assertion

registers.name: auxpll_fuse_fusecr_fusa_0_pll_lockthresh_postlock
registers.description: Post-lock PLL lock detector phase error threshold above which PLL is considered unlocked. Encoding: 0x0 = 40ps, 0x1 = 70ps, 0x2 = 100ps, 0x3 = 130ps, 0x4 = 170ps, 0x5 = 200ps, 0x6 = 240ps, 0x7 = 270ps, 0x8 = 500ps, 0x9 = 800ps, 0xA = 1000ps, 0xB = 1300ps, 0xC = 1550ps, 0xD = 1800ps, 0xE = 2050ps, 0xF = 2300ps

registers.name: auxpll_fuse_fusecr_perf_0_cpi_opamp_enable
registers.description: cpi opamp disable. Encoding: 0x0 = disabled, 0x1=enabled.

registers.name: auxpll_fuse_fusecr_perf_0_ldo1_rcomp_cfg
registers.description: LDO 1 compensation resistor configuration: 0x0 = 1kOhm, 0x1 = 2kOhm, 0x2 = 3kOhm, 0x3 = 4kOhm.

registers.name: auxpll_fuse_fusecr_proc_0_fast_lock_higain_duration
registers.description: Duration for which fast lock high gain settings are applied. Encoding: 0x0 = until EarlyLock, 0x1 = 5 refclks, 0x2 = 10 refclks, 0x3 = 15 refclks.

registers.name: auxpll_fuse_fusecr_proc_0_ldo_vrefsel
registers.description: LDO VREF trim configuration: 0x0 = minimum, 0x9 = maximum, 0xA-0xF = unused

registers.name: auxpll_fuse_fusecr_proc_0_pll_lockthresh_prelock
registers.description: Pre-lock PLL lock detector phase error threshold below which PLL is considered locked. Encoding: 0x0 = 40ps, 0x1 = 70ps, 0x2 = 100ps, 0x3 = 130ps, 0x4 = 170ps, 0x5 = 200ps, 0x6 = 240ps, 0x7 = 270ps, 0x8 = 500ps, 0x9 = 800ps, 0xA = 1000ps, 0xB = 1300ps, 0xC = 1550ps, 0xD = 1800ps, 0xE = 2050ps, 0xF = 2300ps

registers.name: auxpll_fuse_fusecr_proc_0_startup_rdac
registers.description: startup RDAC output voltage divider, used to set VCO startup frequency for lower ratios (linear steps): 0x0 = max_freq (0.25V), 0xF = min_freq (0.75V)

registers.name: auxpll_fuse_fusecr_proc_0_startup_rdac_hifreq

registers.name: auxpll_fuse_fusecr_proc_0_vco_trim_pg

registers.name: bclkpll_top_fusecr_perf_0_cpi_opamp_enable
registers.description: cpi opamp disable. Encoding: 0x0 = disabled, 0x1=enabled.

registers.name: bclkpll_top_fusecr_perf_0_freq_crawl_dn_delta_f

registers.name: bclkpll_top_fusecr_perf_0_freq_crawl_dn_delta_t
registers.description: Freqency crawl down duration between ratio steps in refclk cycles. Encoding: 0x0=1 cycle, 0x1=2 cycles, 0x2=4 cycles, 0x3=6 cycles, 0x4=8 cycles, 0x5=16 cycles, 0x6=24 cycles, 0x7=32 cycles, 0x8-0xF=128 cycles.

registers.name: bclkpll_top_fusecr_perf_0_freq_crawl_up_delta_f

registers.name: bclkpll_top_fusecr_perf_0_ldo1_rcomp_cfg

registers.description: Integral charge pump current setting: 0x0 = min, 0x1F = max

registers.name: bclkpll_top_fusecr_proc_1_iref_trim
registers.description: IREF current strength tuning. Encoding: 0x0 = min strength, 0x1F = max strength

registers.name: bclkpll_top_ssc_control_ssc_cyc_to_peak_m1
registers.description: SSC cycles to peak minus 1; half the number of steps in the modulation period minus 1. Period of modulation is 2*(value+1) multiplied by the step duration (PLL refclk period). eg 0x257 => 2*(599+1) * (1/38.4MHz) = 600 cycles * 52.083ns = 31.25us. Spread magnitude is determined by the fractional ratio step size multiplied by cyc_to_peak_m1 + 1 (see ssc_frac_step for ratio step size).

registers.name: bclkpll_top_ssc_control_ssc_frac_step
registers.description: Spread Spectrum Clocking: fractional step configuration; fraction of PLL ratio at which to take frequency modulation steps. Frac_step = PLL ratio * % spread * 2^(num frac bits) / cycles_to_peak. Example for 0.46% spread amplitude and 32kHz period with PLL ratio of 800MHz/38.4MHz and a 22-bit fractional ratio: 20.833 * 0.0046 * 2^22 / 600 = 670 or 0x29E (see cyc_to_peak_m1 to calculate spread period).

registers.name: bclkpll_top_ssc_control_ssc_mode
registers.description: Spread Spectrum Clocking: SSC spread direction; 0x0 = disabled; 0x1 = down spread; 0x2 = center spread ; 0x3 = up spread
