registers.name: ddrphy_fuse_ddrcomp_cr_ugbcontrol_ugbrampupdelay
registers.description: Delay from Vccdd2G PowerUp to UGBEnable to prevent forward bias on DAC power mux: Delay = (UGBRampUpDelay*4 SBClk).  Both circuits turn off together.

registers.name: ddrphy_fuse_ddrcomp_cr_ugbcontrol_vccclkslopeoc
registers.description: VccCLK Slope for DDR5 Overclocking in units of (One 2.604mV step per 400 MTs).  These values are indended as a reasonable starting point and not guaranteed since they are characterized on a limited sampling of parts.

registers.name: ddrphy_fuse_ddrcomp_cr_ugbcontrol_vccioslopeoc
registers.description: VccIO Slope for DDR5 Overclocking in units of (One 2.604mV step per 400 MTs).  These values are intended as a reasonable starting point and not guaranteed since they are characterized on a limited sampling of parts.

registers.name: ddrphy_fuse_ddrdatashared_cr_cbtune0_bw0
registers.description: DLL Bandwidth setting for (PHclkRatio33 < 35)

registers.name: ddrphy_fuse_ddrdatashared_cr_cbtune0_bw1
registers.description: DLL Bandwidth setting for (35 <= PHclkRatio33 < 41)

registers.name: ddrphy_fuse_ddrdatashared_cr_cbtune0_bw2
registers.description: DLL Bandwidth setting for (41 <= PHclkRatio33 < 49)

registers.name: ddrphy_fuse_ddrdatashared_cr_cbtune0_bw3
registers.description: DLL Bandwidth setting for (49 <= PHclkRatio33 < 57)

registers.name: ddrphy_fuse_ddrdatashared_cr_cbtune0_bw4
registers.description: DLL Bandwidth setting for (57 <= PHclkRatio33 < 67)

registers.name: ddrphy_fuse_ddrdatashared_cr_cbtune0_bw5
registers.description: DLL Bandwidth setting for (67 <= PHclkRatio33 < 79)

registers.name: ddrphy_fuse_ddrdatashared_cr_cbtune1_bw6
registers.description: DLL Bandwidth setting for (79 <= PHclkRatio33 < 93)

registers.name: ddrphy_fuse_ddrdatashared_cr_cbtune1_bw7
registers.description: DLL Bandwidth setting for (93 <= PHclkRatio33 < 110)

registers.name: ddrphy_fuse_ddrdatashared_cr_cbtune1_bw8
registers.description: DLL Bandwidth setting for (110 <= PHclkRatio33)

registers.name: ddrphy_fuse_ddrdatashared_cr_cbtune1_cb0
registers.description: DLL CB setting for (PHclkRatio33 < 35)

registers.name: ddrphy_fuse_ddrdatashared_cr_cbtune1_cb1
registers.description: DLL CB setting for (35 <= PHclkRatio33 < 41)

registers.name: ddrphy_fuse_ddrdatashared_cr_cbtune1_cb2
registers.description: DLL CB setting for (41 <= PHclkRatio33 < 49)

registers.name: ddrphy_fuse_ddrdatashared_cr_cbtune1_cb3
registers.description: DLL CB setting for (49 <= PHclkRatio33 < 57)

registers.name: ddrphy_fuse_ddrdatashared_cr_cbtune1_cb4
registers.description: DLL CB setting for (57 <= PHclkRatio33 < 67)

registers.name: ddrphy_fuse_ddrdatashared_cr_cbtune1_cb5
registers.description: DLL CB setting for (67 <= PHclkRatio33 < 79)

registers.name: ddrphy_fuse_ddrdatashared_cr_cbtune1_cb6
registers.description: DLL CB setting for (79 <= PHclkRatio33 < 93)

registers.name: ddrphy_fuse_ddrpll_cr_ddrcrcomppll_phclkpllfeedback
registers.description: PhClk PLL phase feedback uses (0: QClk Spine, 1: Pure Tight Loop, 2: Delayed tight loop, 3: rsvd).  AuxClk always using PLLFuseAuxClk.DeskewEn.

registers.name: ddrphy_fuse_ddrpll_cr_ddrcrcomppll_tldelay
registers.description: Tight Loop delay generated by FSM or manually programmed (TLFsmEn=0)

registers.name: ddrphy_fuse_ddrpll_cr_ddrcrcomppll_tlfsmen
registers.description: Tight loop FSM runs (0: Only until lock is detector, 1: Continously, 2: Sleeps for 32 PLL Ref Clock after an update, 3: Sleeps for 64 PLL Ref Clocks after an update).  In state 2 or 3, FSM can't stay in count for more than Max(1 Update, 4 PLL RefClocks)

registers.name: ddrphy_fuse_ddrpll_cr_ddrcrcomppll_tlfsmstart
registers.description: Tight loop FSM starts after (0: PLL Lock, 1: 64 RefClock after PLLEnable, 2: 128 RefClock after PLLEnable, , 1: 192 RefClock after PLLEnable)

registers.name: ddrphy_fuse_ddrpll_cr_ddrcrcomppll_tlrstdelay
registers.description: Tight loop FSM starts from a delay of (0: 0x0, 1: Prior TLDelay)

registers.name: ddrphy_fuse_mcmiscssaug_cr_pg_ctrl2_distg_lvrdisabledelay
registers.description: DISTG LVR : Power Good Deassert to actual DistG LVR disable Delay; 2^(1 + DistG_LVRDisableDelay) Sbcycles

registers.name: ddrphy_fuse_mcmiscssaug_cr_pg_ctrl2_distg_rampupdelay
registers.description: DISTG LVR : LVR Enable to Power Good assertion delay (supply to stabalize + Power Good assert delay); (0x0:250ns, 0x1:300ns, 0x2:400ns, 0x3:600ns) 100, 120, 160, 240 SBclk cycles respectively

registers.name: ddrphy_fuse_mcmiscssaug_cr_pg_ctrl2_vccddqgpwrgooddelay
registers.description: VccDDQG : Delay from ForceOn to VccIOGPwrGood assert: 2^(2+VccDDQGPwrGoodDelay) SB clocks

registers.name: ddrphy_fuse_mcmiscssaug_cr_pg_ctrl2_vccddqgrampup
registers.description: VccDDQG : Delay from PowerUp to ForceOn (VCC ramp up Delay): 0x0 - 40ns, 0x1 - 80ns, 0x2 - 120ns, 0x3 - 160ns,  requiement is 120ns

registers.name: ddrphy_fuse_mcmiscssaug_cr_pg_ctrl2_vcciogpwrgooddelay
registers.description: VccIOG : Delay from ForceOn to VccIOGPwrGood assert: 2^(2+VccIOGPwrGoodDelay) SB clocks

registers.name: ddrphy_fuse_mcmiscssaug_cr_pg_ctrl2_vcciogrampup
registers.description: VccIOG : Delay from PullUp Enable to ForceOn (VccIOG ramp up Delay):  0x0 - 40ns, 0x1 - 80ns, 0x2 - 120ns, 0x3 - 160ns. Requirement is 120ns.

registers.name: ddrphy_fuse_mcmiscssaug_cr_pg_ctrl2_vccsaxgpwrgooddelay
registers.description: Delay from ForceOn to VccXXPwrGood assert: 2^(2+SAXGPwrGoodDelay) SB clocks

registers.name: ddrphy_fuse_mcmiscssaug_cr_pg_ctrl2_vccsaxgrampup
registers.description: Delay from Pullup-Enable to ForceOn (VCC ramp up delay): 0x0 - 40ns, 0x1 - 80ns, 0x2 - 120ns, 0x3 - 160ns, 0x4 - 200ns, 0x5 - 320ns,  0x6 - 640ns, 0x7 - 1280ns. Requirement is 120ns.

registers.name: ddrphy_fuse_mcmiscssaug_cr_pg_lvrrampdn
registers.description: Delay from DisableVR (Vddq, IO, CLK) to FSM continues to the next state, allowing supply time to ramp down:  : 2^(5+LVRRampDown) SB clocks

registers.name: ddrphy_fuse_mcmiscssaug_cr_pg_pgramprate
registers.description: VDD2G PG FSM walks 1 DAC steps every (1+PGRampRate) SB Clock during power up, where each DAC steps to Vcc1p8/32

registers.name: ddrphy_fuse_mcmiscssaug_cr_pg_vccsagpwrgooddelay
registers.description: Delay from ForceOn to VccXXPwrGood assert: 2^(2+SAGPwrGoodDelay) SB clocks

registers.name: ddrphy_fuse_mcmiscssaug_cr_pg_vccsagrampup
registers.description: Delay from (FSM counter reaches 32) to ForceOn: 2^(2+Vdd2GRampUp) SB clocks

registers.name: ddrphy_fuse_mcmiscssaug_cr_pg_vdd2gpwrgooddelay
registers.description: Delay from Force On to VccXXPwrGood assert: 2^(2+Vdd2PwrGoodDelay) SB clocks

registers.name: ddrphy_fuse_mcmiscssaug_cr_pg_vdd2grampup
registers.description: Delay from (FSM counter reaches 32) to ForceOn: 2^(2+Vdd2GRampUp) SB clocks

registers.name: ddrphy_fuse_mcmiscssaug_cr_ucsramtrim_rftrim_mce
registers.description: UC rf trime fuse mce

registers.name: ddrphy_fuse_mcmiscssaug_cr_ucsramtrim_rftrim_rmce
registers.description: UC rf trime fuse rmce

registers.name: ddrphy_fuse_mcmiscssaug_cr_ucsramtrim_rftrim_stbyp
registers.description: UC rf trime fuse stbyp

registers.name: ddrphy_fuse_mcmiscssaug_cr_ucsramtrim_rftrim_wmce
registers.description: UC rf trime fusewmce

registers.name: ddrphy_fuse_mcmiscssaug_cr_ucsramtrim_sramtrim_mce
registers.description: UC sram trime fuse mce

registers.name: ddrphy_fuse_mcmiscssaug_cr_ucsramtrim_sramtrim_rmce
registers.description: UC sram trime fuse rmce

registers.name: ddrphy_fuse_mcmiscssaug_cr_ucsramtrim_sramtrim_wa
registers.description: UC sram trime fuse wa

registers.name: ddrphy_fuse_mcmiscssaug_cr_ucsramtrim_sramtrim_wmce
registers.description: UC sram trime fuse wmce

registers.name: ddrphy_fuse_mcmiscssaug_cr_vccclkrange_vccf0
registers.description: VccF0, VccF1 and VccF2 together repurposed to DDRMCMISC_CR_IPVERSION_0_0_0_MCHBAR CR.

registers.name: ddrphy_fuse_mcmiscssaug_cr_vccclkrange_vccf1
registers.description: VccF0, VccF1 and VccF2 together repurposed to DDRMCMISC_CR_IPVERSION_0_0_0_MCHBAR CR.

registers.name: de_audio_codec_id
registers.description:
SKUing: Set the lower 8 bits of the audio codec ID
DFSM, 0x51000[15:8]

registers.name: de_dg_parity_dni_dis
registers.description: Disable Parity generation/checking on DNI

registers.name: de_dg_parity_iosf_dis
registers.description: Disable Parity generation/checking on IOSF

registers.name: de_piped_dis
registers.description:
SKUing: Disable pipe D capability
DFSM, 0x51000[22]

registers.name: de_pipee_dis
registers.description:
Not supported. This fuse must be set to 1. 
SKUing: Disable pipe E capability
DFSM, 0x51000[24]

registers.name: de_pipef_dis
registers.description:
Not supported. This fuse must be set to 1. 
SKUing: Disable pipe F capability
DFSM, 0x51000[27]

registers.name: de_trim_hd2prf_mce
registers.description:
Margin Control Enable
[0]  -  Safe TM setting
[1]  -  TM controlled by rmce, wmce

registers.name: de_trim_hd2prf_rmce
registers.description:
Controls internal read/write clock pulse width
[00]  -  TM0 Safe timing mode
[01]  -  TM1
[10]  -  TM2
[11]  -  TM3

registers.name: de_trim_hd2prf_stbyp
registers.description:
Debug mode
[0]  -  IP generated self time clock used for R/W
[1]  -  External clock used for R/W (for debug and HVQK/BI)

registers.name: de_trim_hd2prf_wmce
registers.description:
Controls internal read/write clock pulse width
[00]  -  TM0 Safe timing mode
[01]  -  TM1
[10]  -  TM2
[11]  -  TM3

registers.name: de_trim_hdspsram_mce
registers.description:
Margin Control Enable
[0]  -  Hardwired to optimal read/write margin setting
[1]  -  Disables overrides, allows *mce pins to be toggled

registers.name: de_trim_hdspsram_ra
registers.description:
Controls WL voltage droop during Read Assist.
[00]  -  Strongest Assist
[01]  -  Strong Assist
[10]  -  Weak Assist
[11]  -  Weakest/No Assist/TD recommended setting

registers.name: de_trim_hdspsram_rmce
registers.description:
Controls sense-amp enable timing in debug mode
[0000]  -  Slowest tracker setting with largest SA differentia
 [1111]  -  Fastest tracker setting with smallest SA
differential

registers.name: de_trim_hdspsram_wa
registers.description:
wa[2] - Enables/disables clamp logic to reduce NBL level at high voltage operations. 
[0] - Disables NBL clamp
[1] - Enables NBL clamp
wa[1:0] - Controls NBL level during Write Assist.
[00]  -  NBL level meeting TD spec at Vmin
[01]  -  Added metal cap only
[10]  -  Added device + metal cap
[11]  -  Max device + metal gap

registers.name: de_trim_hdspsram_wmce
registers.description:
Controls WLKILL/RCVR timing
[00] - Enables longest delay
[01] - Enables shortest delay
[10/11] - Enables intermediate delay

registers.name: default_personality_fuse_enable
registers.description:
If DPFE is 4'b1010, DPFV will be reflected as default value at personality generator output(SuperSKU Unlock). _x000D_
Any values other than 4'b1010 will not enable SuperSKU Unlock._x000D_
Default value is 4'b0101.

registers.name: default_personality_fuse_value
registers.description: If DPFE is 4'b1010, DPFV will be reflected as default value at personality generator output. If DPFE is not equal to 4'b1010, then DPFV value will not be considered.

registers.name: depll_fuse_proxy_fusecr_app_0_pll_gv_vid_boost_disable
registers.description: PLL mode FGV VID boost disable. Encoding: 0x0 = boost request enabled, 0x1 = boost request disabled.

registers.name: depll_fuse_proxy_fusecr_app_0_tllm_en
registers.description: Tight loop lock mode enable; 0x0 = Disable tight loop lock mode; 0x1 = enable tight loop lock mode

registers.name: depll_fuse_proxy_fusecr_app_0_tllm_output_gating
registers.description: Tight loop lock mode distribution clock gating control. Encoding: 0x0 = gate clock until final lock (tight-loop feedback and static DCC) or early lock (long-loop feedback or active DCC); 0x1 = gate clock until early lock; 0x2 = reserved; 0x3 = no clock gating during lock acquisition.

registers.name: depll_fuse_proxy_fusecr_dcc_0_dcs_offset_dca_static
registers.description: Duty cycle offset target with DCS enabled; static duty cycle setting with DCS enabled; 0x00=minimum high phase, 0x20=center setting, 0x3F=maximum high phase

registers.name: depll_fuse_proxy_fusecr_fusa_0_fll_lockthresh_postlock
registers.description: Frequency based FLL lock detector threshold for FuSa defined as allowed percentage error of target frequency count. Encoding: 0x0 = 0.75%, 0x1 = 1.5%, 0x2 = 3.0%, 0x3 = 6.0%.

registers.name: depll_fuse_proxy_fusecr_fusa_0_fusa_unlockerr_cfg
registers.description: FuSa unlock error indicator feature enable and reset condition: 0x0=disabled, 0x1=reset on side_rst, 0x2=reset on PLL disable, 0x3=reset on power-good de-assertion

registers.name: depll_fuse_proxy_fusecr_fusa_0_pll_lockthresh_postlock
registers.description: Post-lock PLL lock detector phase error threshold above which PLL is considered unlocked. Encoding: 0x0 = 40ps, 0x1 = 70ps, 0x2 = 100ps, 0x3 = 130ps, 0x4 = 170ps, 0x5 = 200ps, 0x6 = 240ps, 0x7 = 270ps, 0x8 = 500ps, 0x9 = 800ps, 0xA = 1000ps, 0xB = 1300ps, 0xC = 1550ps, 0xD = 1800ps, 0xE = 2050ps, 0xF = 2300ps

registers.name: depll_fuse_proxy_fusecr_perf_0_cpi_opamp_enable
registers.description: cpi opamp disable. Encoding: 0x0 = disabled, 0x1=enabled.

registers.name: depll_fuse_proxy_fusecr_perf_0_freq_ack_interrupt_latency
registers.description: Interrupted frequency crawl ack pulse width in pre-div refclk cycles. Encoding: 0x0 = 4 cycles,  0x1 = 8 cycles, 0x2 = 16 cycles,  0x3 = 32 cycles

registers.name: depll_fuse_proxy_fusecr_perf_0_freq_crawl_dn_delta_f
registers.description: Frequency crawl down ratio step size. Encoding: 0x0=0.5 ratio, 0x1=1 ratio, 0x2=2 ratios, 0x3=3 ratios.

registers.name: depll_fuse_proxy_fusecr_perf_0_freq_crawl_dn_delta_t
registers.description: Freqency crawl down duration between ratio steps in refclk cycles. Encoding: 0x0=1 cycle, 0x1=2 cycles, 0x2=4 cycles, 0x3=6 cycles, 0x4=8 cycles, 0x5=16 cycles, 0x6=24 cycles, 0x7=32 cycles, 0x8-0xF=128 cycles.

registers.name: depll_fuse_proxy_fusecr_perf_0_freq_crawl_up_delta_f
registers.description: Frequency crawl up ratio step size. Encoding: 0x0=0.5 ratio, 0x1=1 ratio, 0x2=2 ratios, 0x3=3 ratios.

registers.name: depll_fuse_proxy_fusecr_perf_0_freq_crawl_up_delta_t
registers.description: Freqency crawl up duration between ratio steps in refclk cycles. Encoding: 0x0=1 cycle, 0x1=2 cycles, 0x2=4 cycles, 0x3=6 cycles, 0x4=8 cycles, 0x5=16 cycles, 0x6=24 cycles, 0x7=32 cycles, 0x8-0xF=128 cycles.

registers.name: depll_fuse_proxy_fusecr_perf_0_ldo1_rcomp_cfg
registers.description: LDO 1 compensation resistor configuration: 0x0 = 1kOhm, 0x1 = 2kOhm, 0x2 = 3kOhm, 0x3 = 4kOhm.

registers.name: depll_fuse_proxy_fusecr_proc_0_fast_lock_higain_duration
registers.description: Duration for which fast lock high gain settings are applied. Encoding: 0x0 = until EarlyLock, 0x1 = 5 refclks, 0x2 = 10 refclks, 0x3 = 15 refclks.

registers.name: depll_fuse_proxy_fusecr_proc_0_pll_lockthresh_prelock
registers.description: Pre-lock PLL lock detector phase error threshold below which PLL is considered locked. Encoding: 0x0 = 40ps, 0x1 = 70ps, 0x2 = 100ps, 0x3 = 130ps, 0x4 = 170ps, 0x5 = 200ps, 0x6 = 240ps, 0x7 = 270ps, 0x8 = 500ps, 0x9 = 800ps, 0xA = 1000ps, 0xB = 1300ps, 0xC = 1550ps, 0xD = 1800ps, 0xE = 2050ps, 0xF = 2300ps

registers.name: depll_fuse_proxy_fusecr_proc_0_startup_rdac
registers.description: startup RDAC output voltage divider, used to set VCO startup frequency for lower ratios (linear steps): 0x0 = max_freq (0.25V), 0xF = min_freq (0.75V)

registers.name: depll_fuse_proxy_fusecr_proc_0_startup_rdac_hifreq
registers.description: startup RDAC output voltage divider, used to set VCO startup frequency for higher ratios (linear steps): 0x0 = max_freq (0.25V), 0xF = min_freq (0.75V)

registers.name: depll_fuse_proxy_fusecr_proc_0_vco_trim_pg
registers.description: VCO RO powergate trim affecting VCO gain: 0x0 = min (6GHz/V), 0xF = max (26GHz/V)

registers.name: depll_fuse_proxy_fusecr_proc_1_cpi_trim
registers.description: Integral charge pump current setting: 0x0 = min, 0x1F = max

registers.name: depll_fuse_proxy_fusecr_proc_1_cpi_trim_fastlock
registers.description: Integral charge pump current setting for fast lock: 0x0 = min, 0x1F = max

registers.name: depll_fuse_proxy_fusecr_proc_1_cpp_trim
registers.description: Proportional charge pump strength: 0x0 = min, 0x1F = max

registers.name: depll_fuse_proxy_fusecr_proc_1_cpp_trim_fastlock
registers.description: Proportional charge pump current setting for fast lock: 0x0 = min, 0x1F = max

registers.name: depll_fuse_proxy_fusecr_proc_1_iref2_nlvcascode
registers.description: Irefgen2 mode to linearize output current v/s VCO virtual supply

registers.name: depll_fuse_proxy_fusecr_proc_1_iref_trim
registers.description: IREF current strength tuning. Encoding: 0x0 = min strength, 0x1F = max strength

registers.name: dfdpll_top_fusecr_dcc_0_dcs_offset_dca_static
registers.description: Duty cycle offset target with DCS enabled; static duty cycle setting with DCS enabled; 0x00=minimum high phase, 0x20=center setting, 0x3F=maximum high phase

registers.name: dfdpll_top_fusecr_fll_perf_0_det_lock_count
registers.description: Deterministic lock time duration defined as number of linear search measurements after binary search completion. Does not apply to pre-calibration or dynamic frequency changes. Encoding: 0x0 = 0, 0x1 = 4, 0x2 =8 , 0x3 = 16.

registers.name: dfdpll_top_fusecr_fll_perf_0_gv_afc_damping
registers.description: AFC GV ramp rate damping and proportional step size in unconstrained ramp rate mode. Encoding: 0x0 = lowest damping (Err/2), 0x1 = lower damping (Err/4), 0x2 = higher damping (Err/8), 0x3 = highest damping (Err/16).

registers.name: dfdpll_top_fusecr_fll_perf_0_gv_afc_exit_err
registers.description: AFC GV frequency error exit criteria relative to target count with offset given by gv_target_offset fuse. Encoding: 0x0 = lower error (up to 3 DCO codes), 0x1 = higher error (up to 7 DCO codes).

registers.name: dfdpll_top_fusecr_fll_perf_0_gv_afc_ramp_rate
registers.description: AFC GV frequency ramp rate in approximate GHz/us slope. Encoding: 0x0 = unconstrained, 0x1 = 0.25, 0x2 = 0.33, 0x3 = 0.45, 0x4 = 0.60, 0x5 = 0.75, 0x6 = 0.90, 0x7 = 1.1, 0x8 = 1.4, 0x9 = 1.8, 0xA = 2.3, 0xB = 2.9, 0xC = 3.6, 0xD = 4.7, 0xE = 6.6, 0xF = 9.4

registers.name: dfdpll_top_fusecr_fll_perf_0_raw_lock_thresh
registers.description: Frequency based lock detector threshold defined as allowed percentage error of target frequency count. Encoding: 0x0 = 0.75%, 0x1 = 1.5%, 0x2 = 3.0%, 0x3 = 6.0%.

registers.name: dfdpll_top_fusecr_fll_proc_0_dco_ireftune
registers.description: FLL DCOA IREF strength. Encoding: 0x0 = minimum strength, 0xF = maximum strength.

registers.name: dfdpll_top_fusecr_fll_proc_0_dco_settle_time
registers.description: Duration allocated to DCO settling after applying a code update prior to starting next measurement. Encoding: 0x0 = 10ns, 0x1 = 15ns, 0x2 = 20ns, 0x3 = 30ns, 0x4 = 35ns, 0x5 = 40ns, 0x6 = 50ns, 0x7 = 65ns.

registers.name: dfdpll_top_fusecr_fll_proc_0_gv_target_offset
registers.description: Frequency counter target offset to avoid overshoot during GV. FGV encoding: 0x0 = -1 count, 0x1 = -2 counts, 0x2 = -3 counts, 0x3 = -4 counts. AFC (SGV) encoding: 0x0 = -1 count, 0x1 = -3 counts, 0x2 = -5 counts, 0x3 = -7 counts.

registers.name: dfdpll_top_fusecr_fusa_0_fll_lockthresh_postlock
registers.description: Frequency based FLL lock detector threshold for FuSa defined as allowed percentage error of target frequency count. Encoding: 0x0 = 0.75%, 0x1 = 1.5%, 0x2 = 3.0%, 0x3 = 6.0%.

registers.name: dfdpll_top_fusecr_fusa_0_fusa_unlockerr_cfg
registers.description: FuSa unlock error indicator feature enable and reset condition: 0x0=disabled, 0x1=reset on side_rst, 0x2=reset on PLL disable, 0x3=reset on power-good de-assertion

registers.name: dfdpll_top_fusecr_fusa_0_pll_lockthresh_postlock
registers.description: Post-lock PLL lock detector phase error threshold above which PLL is considered unlocked. Encoding: 0x0 = 40ps, 0x1 = 70ps, 0x2 = 100ps, 0x3 = 130ps, 0x4 = 170ps, 0x5 = 200ps, 0x6 = 240ps, 0x7 = 270ps, 0x8 = 500ps, 0x9 = 800ps, 0xA = 1000ps, 0xB = 1300ps, 0xC = 1550ps, 0xD = 1800ps, 0xE = 2050ps, 0xF = 2300ps

registers.name: dfdpll_top_fusecr_perf_0_cpi_opamp_enable
registers.description: cpi opamp disable. Encoding: 0x0 = disabled, 0x1=enabled.

registers.name: dfdpll_top_fusecr_perf_0_ldo1_rcomp_cfg
registers.description: LDO 1 compensation resistor configuration: 0x0 = 1kOhm, 0x1 = 2kOhm, 0x2 = 3kOhm, 0x3 = 4kOhm.

registers.name: dfdpll_top_fusecr_proc_0_vco_trim_pg
registers.description: VCO RO powergate trim affecting VCO gain: 0x0 = min (6GHz/V), 0xF = max (26GHz/V)

registers.name: dlcs_manufacturing_exit
registers.description: Manufacturing_exit_fuse when 1, DLCS is in Production

registers.name: dts_fuse_active_diode_mask
registers.description: Selected remote diodes that participate in the temperature measurement. Each bit represent one diode, a set value of 1 denotes the diode is enabled

registers.name: dts_fuse_adc_buf_interim_prog_delay
registers.description: Programmable interim Adc buffer enable delay

registers.name: dts_fuse_adc_integ_rst_pulse

registers.name: dts_fuse_rdcal_mask1

registers.description: DISP ENG ID. (GMD Arch ID for Display)

registers.name: fw_fuses_bclk_gov_en
registers.description:
Enable Bclk Governor mechanism.
            This mechanism checks if Bclk (possibly overclocked) frequency is in allowed range.
            The range is defined by the fuse BCLK_OC_RANGE

registers.name: fw_fuses_boot_nocpll_ratio
registers.description: Boot Main NOC PLL ratio (in 50MHz granularity) - 550 MHz

registers.name: fw_fuses_boot_ucie_d2d_lclk_ratio
registers.description: Boot D2D PLL ratio (in 100MHz granularity) - 1GHz

registers.name: fw_fuses_cachesz
registers.description:
LLC size/ways
                (Once it was upgradable through SSKU up to LLC_WAY_EN.) Fuse value
                LLC Size per slice (Enabled ways per slice)
                5b fuse value represents the number of ways
                Valid encodings in LNL are every number between  - 2 and 12. Max number of ways supported is 12.
                LLC size/slice = fuse_encoding * 0.25MB (12 ways->3M)

registers.name: fw_fuses_capid0b_bit28
registers.description:
bit 28 in CAPID0_B is !HT_DIS=SMT_ENABLED which is taken from other fuse.
                         This fuse is here as a placeholder for the bit in CAPID0_B, it is not spare/reserved!
                         Its value is ignored, and bit 28 in CAPIDB is overridden with !HT_DIS (which is actually SMT_EN).

registers.name: fw_fuses_cdie_dash
registers.description: Indicates the CDIE silicon DASH number

registers.name: fw_fuses_core_c_state
registers.description:
Max core C_state allowed; Will be used to set the limit in the PCU, clipping the
                Wish-request for uCode (via the IO_DEMOTE_TO_C1 register) Loaded into
                PACKAGE_CONFIG_CR.CORE_C_STATE_LIMIT by PCode at reset Used by uCode as well to report the
                max number.

registers.name: fw_fuses_crid
registers.description:
4-bit fuse that indicates the LSB of the Compatible Revision ID revision identification of
                the product (4 MSB's are always zero).  Normally, the value assigned as the CRID will be
                identical to the SRID value till PRQ.  After PRQ CRID could be used for silent steppings.

registers.name: fw_fuses_d2crid
registers.description: Indicates the LSB of the Compatible Revision ID revision identification of Device 2 (4 MSB's are always zero). Normally, the value assigned as the D2CRID will be identical to the D2SRID value of a previous stepping of the product with which the new product is deemed compatible, but multiple D2CRID values are possible. RID and is reflected through the RID register located at offset 08h in the PCI header of every PCI device and function, when appropriately selected by BIOS.

registers.name: fw_fuses_d2srid
registers.description: Indicates the LSB of the Stepping Revision ID revision identification of Device 2 (4 MSB's are hardwired). SRID is reflected through the RID register located at offset 08h in the PCI header of every PCI device and function, unless BIOS selected CRID to be reflected.

registers.name: fw_fuses_ddpcd
registers.description:
DDR Double DIMM Per Channel Disable
                Specifies the allowed DIMMs per Channel
                0: 2 DIMMs per channel enabled
                1: restricted to single DIMM per channel.
                This setting hardwires bits 2 and 3 of the rank population field for each channel to zero.
                Only applicable to DDR5.

registers.name: fw_fuses_ddr5_dsku_allowed
registers.description:
0: Not allow dsku override on CAPID0_E.DDR5_EN
                1: Allow dsku override on CAPID0_E.DDR5_EN

registers.name: fw_fuses_ddr5_en
registers.description: Allow DDR5 operation.

registers.name: fw_fuses_ddr_slice_dis
registers.description: DDR Memory Slice Disable. 0: Both Memory slices can be enabled. 1: Memory Slice 0 is disabled. 2: Memory Slice 1 is disabled.

registers.name: fw_fuses_did2_bit7
registers.description:
This is an extra bit for DE only. The low 7 lsbs in the DID (9 project related, 7 device
                specific) are not enough to describe all DEV2 skus, so we need an extra bit. But DE can't
                add this fuse, so pcode is adding and write it as part of setIDValue message.
      
                Note: this fuse should be moved to DE, punit should not own it.

registers.name: fw_fuses_display_piped_dis
registers.description: Display 4th Pipe Disable (new in ICL)

registers.name: fw_fuses_do_not_exceed_temp
registers.description: Temperature that the SoC should not exceed. Distributed to HW protectors like Thermal HW Assist and ACP Hammer Throttle.

registers.name: fw_fuses_dtf_enable
registers.description:
If clear, the punit trace to DTF (debug trace fabric) path is disabled by pcode at reset.
              If set, pcode will enable the path for punit trace to DTF.

registers.name: fw_fuses_eccdis
registers.description:
0: ECC capable
                1: Not ECC capable.
      
                Dependency: If FDEE and ECCDIS are both '1', FDEE takes priority and the part is forced to use ECC for DRAM

registers.name: fw_fuses_ecore_l2_nbl_threshold
registers.description:
Voltage threshold for NBL, above this voltage, NBL is disabled.
                          Value of zero means no dynamic NBL (NBL is always active).

registers.name: fw_fuses_extended_model
registers.description: Extended Model

registers.name: fw_fuses_family_id
registers.description: Family ID

registers.name: fw_fuses_fast_v_mode_enable_gt
registers.description: This fuse determines if the FAST V MODE is enabled for IA svid rail

registers.name: fw_fuses_fast_v_mode_enable_ia
registers.description: This fuse determines if the FAST V MODE is enabled for IA svid rail

registers.name: fw_fuses_fb_hw_sched_intf_en
registers.description: Enable Hetero OS Scheduling Interrupt

registers.name: fw_fuses_ht_dis
registers.description: Disable Multi threading fuse

registers.name: fw_fuses_ht_smt4_dis
registers.description: Disable SMT4 threading fuse

registers.name: fw_fuses_hwp_enable
registers.description: HWP enable, reset value for MISC_PWR_MGMT.ENABLE_HWP

registers.name: fw_fuses_hwp_interrupt_enable
registers.description: HWP interrupt enable, reset value for MISC_PWR_MGMT.ENABLE_HWP_INTERRUPT

registers.name: fw_fuses_hwp_peci_support
registers.description: Reflect to ucode whether the PECI control is supported or not.

registers.name: fw_fuses_hwp_voting
registers.description:
When set, CCP will take into account thread HWP requests for threads that have voting rights only (Pcode ignores thread requests if they don't have voting rights).
                         When reset, CCP will take into account all thread HWP requests, even for threads that don't have voting rights.

registers.name: fw_fuses_iax_device_id

registers.name: fw_fuses_iaxpwrup
registers.description: IAX Device and associated memory spaces are 1=accessible.0=disabled

Internal GFX Exist.
            IGFX_EXIST = 0 means internal GFX doesn't exist on die

registers.name: fw_fuses_imgu_dis
registers.description: IPU Device and associated memory spaces are 0=accessible.1=disabled

registers.name: fw_fuses_lp_ecore_l2_nbl_threshold
registers.description:
Voltage threshold for NBL, above this voltage, NBL is disabled.
                          Value of zero means no dynamic NBL (NBL is always active).

registers.name: fw_fuses_lpddr5_en
registers.description: Allow LPDDR5 operation.

registers.name: fw_fuses_lpm_enable

registers.name: fw_fuses_max_ac_loadline_res

registers.name: fw_fuses_max_dc_loadline_res
registers.description: Max DC load line resistance of input from any VR, in U8.8.0 (0.1 mohm)

registers.name: fw_fuses_max_f_ddr5

registers.name: fw_fuses_max_f_qclk
registers.description: This field controls the Max Qclk frequency in 33.33 MHz units

registers.name: fw_fuses_max_nocpll_ratio
registers.description: Maximal Main NOC PLL ratio (in 50MHz granularity) - 1100 MHz

registers.name: fw_fuses_memss_leak
registers.description: Memss domain max leakage

registers.name: fw_fuses_memss_qclk_cdyn_max
registers.description: Active Cdyn in U8.3.5(nF) SA domain to scale with QClk at V=MEMSS domain

registers.name: fw_fuses_memss_safe_gear

registers.name: fw_fuses_min_f_qclk
registers.description: This field controls the Min Qclk frequency in 33.33 MHz units. Also used as boot frequency before training

registers.name: fw_fuses_min_nocpll_ratio
registers.description: Minimal Main NOC PLL ratio (in 50MHz granularity) - 200 MHz

registers.name: fw_fuses_mk_tme_max_keyid_bits
registers.description: Defines max num of keyid bits. See encoding. Distributed to CCF_NCEVENTS_CR_TME_CAPABILITY[MK_TME_MAX_KEYID_BITS]

registers.name: fw_fuses_mk_tme_max_keys
registers.description: Defines max num of keys. See encoding. Distributed to CCF_NCEVENTS_CR_TME_CAPABILITY[MK_TME_MAX_KEYS]

registers.name: fw_fuses_model
registers.description: Model

registers.name: fw_fuses_ocvolt_ovrd_dis
registers.description: Voltage override overclocking is enabled if 0, disabled if 1.

registers.name: fw_fuses_pcd_dash
registers.description: Indicates the PCD silicon DASH number

registers.name: fw_fuses_pcore_mlc_nbl_threshold
registers.description:
Voltage threshold for NBL, above this voltage, NBL is disabled.
                          Value of zero means no dynamic NBL (NBL is always active).

registers.name: fw_fuses_pkg_c_state
registers.description:
Matches the same definition as CST_CONFIG.Pack_LIM[3:0]. Loaded by pCode upon reset to
                PKG_C_STATE_LIMIT_CR.Pack_LIM[3:0] UCode needs to load it intp CLK_CST_CONFIG_CONTROL upon
                reset exit PCode must keep a copy at hand and use it to clip the coordinated core requests
                (SW can overwrite the CST_CONFIG with values over the fused limits)

registers.name: fw_fuses_pltfrm_id
registers.description: Platform ID, used for selecting which Patch to use and also needed by micro-code to report this value to BIOS/OS via RDMSR.

registers.name: fw_fuses_ppin_enable
registers.description: Control the capability of PPIN (Protected Processor Inventory Number) feature, fuse reflected in PLATFORM_INFO MSR (0xCE) bit #23.

registers.name: fw_fuses_prg_tj_offset_en
registers.description:
Programmable TJ Offset Enable.
            0=Programming Not Allowed
            1=Programming Allowed

registers.name: fw_fuses_proc_num_hi
registers.description: proc num hi (see PROC_NUM_LO description)

registers.name: fw_fuses_proc_num_lo
registers.description:
CNL: Following CNL HSD-ES 1304680701: unique random 56 bits fuse (together with PROC_NUM_HI).
                       TGL bugeco 1304690029 removed the RANDOM_ID fuse. SN0/SN1 is computed as follows on TGL
                          SN0  = PROC_NUM[31:0]
                          SN1 =  8'b0 . PROC_NUM[55:32]
      
          LEGACY DESCRIPTION:  proc num lo/hi
          Following discussion with Ofer Nathan (November 30, 2015), this is currently the fuse breakdown:
          [55:52] Fab ID
          [51:48] Year
          [47:42] WW
          [41:32] Parent Lot (same as 'Sequential lot#' in SNB/IVB)
          [31:26] Child Lot (same as 'Silicon ID' in SNB/IVB)
          [25:16] Wafer Number
          [15:8] X Die Location
          [7:0] Y Die Location

registers.name: fw_fuses_proj_did_msb
registers.description:
These are upper 8 bits of DID to be sent to all IPs. This is project specific.
            CNL: 0x5A
            ICL: 0x8A
            TGL: 0x9A
            ADL: 0x46
            LNL: 0x64
            PTL: 0xB0
            WCL: 0xFD
            Mote: The project ID is actually 9 bits, we assume lsb is always zero (to be aligned with DID0_DID_MSB).

registers.name: fw_fuses_psi_ja_unit
registers.description:
Used along with PSI_JA_VALUE to convert CPU power into Tspec. Will be fused based on the
                sku, DT/MOBIL/SERVER

registers.name: fw_fuses_psi_ja_value
registers.description:
Used along with PSU_JA_UNIT to convert CPU power into Tspec. Will be fused based on the
                sku, DT/MOBIL/SERVER

registers.name: fw_fuses_reference_voltage
registers.description: Reference voltage for Leakage and C-Dyn measurements

registers.name: fw_fuses_se_dis

Secure Enclave Disable
                Dependency: 0 only if PPPE_ENABLE=1 andPRMRR_ENABLE=1

registers.name: fw_fuses_seam_dis
registers.description: Secure Arbitration Mode (SEAM) is a new ISA to support TDX (Trusted Domain Extensions)

registers.name: fw_fuses_srid

4-bit fuse that indicates the LSB of the Stepping Revision ID revision identification of the product (4 MSB's are hardwired).
      SRID is reflected through the RID register located at offset 08h in the PCI header of every PCI device and function, unless BIOS selected CRID to be reflected.

registers.name: fw_fuses_stepping_id

registers.name: fw_fuses_svid_voltage_offset
registers.description:
SVID Offset, and added to VID in VID selection.
                         This represents uncertainty in the VID, for example VR ripple and tolerance. Default is 30mV (0x8)

registers.name: fw_fuses_sys_cache_nbl_threshold
registers.description:
Voltage threshold for NBL, above this voltage, NBL is disabled.
                          Value of zero means no dynamic NBL (NBL is always active).

registers.name: fw_fuses_vpupwrup

X2APIC Enabled (Extended APIC) in the core and unCore:
              Extended Interrupt Mode.
                  0: Hardware does not support Extended APIC mode.
                  1: Hardware supports Extended APIC mode
              The fuse impacts x2APIC support indication in CPUID.

registers.name: fw_pers_mask_fuse
registers.description: fw_pers_mask_fuse is used to mask users from unlocking secure_policy through firmware

registers.name: ondd_rf_fuses_stbyp

registers.name: ondd_rf_fuses_wmce

registers.name: punit_fuses_device_id_dev_4

registers.name: qclkpll_fuse_fusecr_app_0_tllm_output_gating
registers.description: Tight loop lock mode distribution clock gating control. Encoding: 0x0 = gate clock until final lock (tight-loop feedback and static DCC) or early lock (long-loop feedback or active DCC); 0x1 = gate clock until early lock; 0x2 = reserved; 0x3 = no clock gating during lock acquisition.

registers.name: qclkpll_fuse_fusecr_dcc_0_dcs_offset_dca_static
registers.description: Duty cycle offset target with DCS enabled; static duty cycle setting with DCS enabled; 0x00=minimum high phase, 0x20=center setting, 0x3F=maximum high phase

registers.name: qclkpll_fuse_fusecr_fusa_0_fll_lockthresh_postlock
registers.description: Frequency based FLL lock detector threshold for FuSa defined as allowed percentage error of target frequency count. Encoding: 0x0 = 0.75%, 0x1 = 1.5%, 0x2 = 3.0%, 0x3 = 6.0%.

registers.name: qclkpll_fuse_fusecr_fusa_0_fusa_unlockerr_cfg
registers.description: FuSa unlock error indicator feature enable and reset condition: 0x0=disabled, 0x1=reset on side_rst, 0x2=reset on PLL disable, 0x3=reset on power-good de-assertion

registers.name: qclkpll_fuse_fusecr_fusa_0_pll_lockthresh_postlock
registers.description: Post-lock PLL lock detector phase error threshold above which PLL is considered unlocked. Encoding: 0x0 = 40ps, 0x1 = 70ps, 0x2 = 100ps, 0x3 = 130ps, 0x4 = 170ps, 0x5 = 200ps, 0x6 = 240ps, 0x7 = 270ps, 0x8 = 500ps, 0x9 = 800ps, 0xA = 1000ps, 0xB = 1300ps, 0xC = 1550ps, 0xD = 1800ps, 0xE = 2050ps, 0xF = 2300ps

registers.name: qclkpll_fuse_fusecr_perf_0_cpi_opamp_enable
registers.description: cpi opamp disable. Encoding: 0x0 = disabled, 0x1=enabled.

registers.name: qclkpll_fuse_fusecr_perf_0_ldo1_rcomp_cfg
registers.description: LDO 1 compensation resistor configuration: 0x0 = 1kOhm, 0x1 = 2kOhm, 0x2 = 3kOhm, 0x3 = 4kOhm.

registers.name: qclkpll_fuse_fusecr_perf_0_shortpath_enable
registers.description: Select shortened output clock path for reduced jitter, bypassing clock throttling feauture. Encoding: 0x0 = normal output clock path, 0x1 = shortened output clock path.

registers.name: qclkpll_fuse_fusecr_proc_0_fast_lock_higain_duration
registers.description: Duration for which fast lock high gain settings are applied. Encoding: 0x0 = until EarlyLock, 0x1 = 5 refclks, 0x2 = 10 refclks, 0x3 = 15 refclks.

registers.name: qclkpll_fuse_fusecr_proc_0_ldo_vrefsel
registers.description: LDO VREF trim configuration: 0x0 = minimum, 0x9 = maximum, 0xA-0xF = unused

registers.name: qclkpll_fuse_fusecr_proc_0_pll_lockthresh_prelock
registers.description: Pre-lock PLL lock detector phase error threshold below which PLL is considered locked. Encoding: 0x0 = 40ps, 0x1 = 70ps, 0x2 = 100ps, 0x3 = 130ps, 0x4 = 170ps, 0x5 = 200ps, 0x6 = 240ps, 0x7 = 270ps, 0x8 = 500ps, 0x9 = 800ps, 0xA = 1000ps, 0xB = 1300ps, 0xC = 1550ps, 0xD = 1800ps, 0xE = 2050ps, 0xF = 2300ps

registers.name: qclkpll_fuse_fusecr_proc_0_startup_rdac
registers.description: startup RDAC output voltage divider, used to set VCO startup frequency for lower ratios (linear steps): 0x0 = max_freq (0.25V), 0xF = min_freq (0.75V)

registers.name: qclkpll_fuse_fusecr_proc_0_startup_rdac_hifreq
registers.description: startup RDAC output voltage divider, used to set VCO startup frequency for higher ratios (linear steps): 0x0 = max_freq (0.25V), 0xF = min_freq (0.75V)

registers.name: qclkpll_fuse_fusecr_proc_0_vco_trim_pg
registers.description: VCO RO powergate trim affecting VCO gain: 0x0 = min (6GHz/V), 0xF = max (26GHz/V)

registers.name: qclkpll_fuse_fusecr_proc_1_cpi_trim
registers.description: Integral charge pump current setting: 0x0 = min, 0x1F = max

registers.name: qclkpll_fuse_fusecr_proc_1_cpi_trim_fastlock
registers.description: Integral charge pump current setting for fast lock: 0x0 = min, 0x1F = max

registers.name: qclkpll_fuse_fusecr_proc_1_cpp_trim
registers.description: Proportional charge pump strength: 0x0 = min, 0x1F = max

registers.name: qclkpll_fuse_fusecr_proc_1_cpp_trim_fastlock
registers.description: Proportional charge pump current setting for fast lock: 0x0 = min, 0x1F = max

registers.name: qclkpll_fuse_fusecr_proc_1_iref2_nlvcascode
registers.description: Irefgen2 mode to linearize output current v/s VCO virtual supply

registers.name: qclkpll_fuse_fusecr_proc_1_iref_trim
registers.description: IREF current strength tuning. Encoding: 0x0 = min strength, 0x1F = max strength

registers.name: qclkpll_fuse_fusecr_surv_0_cp_lp_en
registers.description: Enable charge pump low power mode; 0x0 = Low power mode disabled; 0x1 = Low power mode enabled

registers.name: qclkpll_fuse_fusecr_surv_0_fracn_extra_lock_count
registers.description: Fractional-N extra lock count

registers.name: qclkpll_fuse_fusecr_surv_0_lock_count
registers.description: PLL lock detection stability timer configuration in cycles of reference clock: 0x0 = 4, 0x1 = 6, 0x2 = 8, 0x3 = 16, 0x4 = 24, 0x5 = 32, 0x6 = 48, 0x7 = 63

registers.name: qclkpll_fuse_fusecr_surv_0_vco_cb
registers.description: capacitive tuning for vco; 0x0 = lowest cap; 0x3 = highest cap

registers.description: Qdf_Sspec_Fuse

registers.name: relock_en_fuse

registers.name: stepping_id
registers.description: Used in JTAG tools to differentiate between silicon steppings that share the same IDCODE register value.

registers.name: ult_fuse
registers.description: Unit Level Tracability

registers.name: uniqueid_fuse
registers.description: Unique ID fuse for iSEED server to do the query.

registers.name: xtalclk_freq_mon_freq_mon_cfgcr_freqmoncfg_ratioerrthresh
registers.description: Configures Freq_mon error logic sensitivity. Please refer to freq_mon files -> ratio_errr.xslx for extra detalis on configurations

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_acm_prev_key_fuse
registers.description: Spare fuse now. Will be removed at the earlier convenience

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_aes_disable_fuse
registers.description: This fuse controls enabling (0=enable, 1=disable) of AES instruction set.

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_apx_enable_fuse
registers.description: Feature Fuse to enable Advanced Performance Extensions (APX) feature. This feature will be enabled starting Arctic Wolf (ARW) core, not in Darkmont

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_avx256p_en_fuse
registers.description: AVX10 Enable fuse. Enable AVX256P if set to 1, the feature will be enabled in ARW and later cores, not in Darkmont

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_c6dram_en_fuse
registers.description: Enables ucode support for C6 state offload.

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_c6sram_l2_ecc_checker_test_enable_fuse
registers.description: When this enable is 1, reset ucode will do ECC logic check before kicking off pBIST while ucode BIST is invoked.

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_cldemote_enable_fuse
registers.description: When this is set, it will enable CLDEMOTE instruction.

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_crashdump_all_cores_dump_xq_fuse
registers.description: When this is set, it will signal that all cores should dump the XQ to the crashdump log. Otherwise only the first core will do so.

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_crashdump_enable_lbr_dump_fuse
registers.description: When crashdump is enabled, this fuse will enable the inclusion of the LBRs in the output state.

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_disable_wb_memtype_in_fit_patch_fuse
registers.description: Disables the feature where ucode will force the patch-loads during the patch_at_reset to be WB.

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_dlsm_enable_fuse
registers.description: This fuse is used to control (1=enable, 0=disable) Dynamic Lockstep Mode feature.

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_enable_ic_init_2_chunks_fuse
registers.description: When this fuse is set to 1, Front End array initialization will get split into two chunks with two largest arrays (UC and BTM) initialized in seperate halves

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_enqcmd_enable_fuse
registers.description: Enables ENQCM(S) instructions as well as IA32_PASID MSR support if set to 1

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_fit_boot_disable_fuse
registers.description: Disables Firmware Interface Table(FIT) boot support PATCH/ACM loading if set to 1

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_fusa_lsm_en_fuse
registers.description: When this is set, WRMSR to 0x2d8 can enable Lockstep mode with peer core

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_fusa_periodic_mem_bist_en_fuse
registers.description: When this is set, WRMSR to 0x2d6, 2d7 can enable periodic MEM BIST

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_fusa_pll_loss_detect_en_fuse
registers.description: PLL lock loss detection supported when set to 1

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_fusa_pwr_loss_detect_en_fuse
registers.description: Power loss detection supported when set to 1

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_fusa_scan_at_field_en_fuse
registers.description: When this is set,  enable SAF

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_fusa_startup_mem_bist_en_fuse
registers.description: When this is set, WRMSR to 0x2d4,2d5 can enable startup MEM BIST

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_fusa_startup_scan_bist_en_fuse
registers.description: When this is set, WRMSR to 0x2d2,2d3 can enable startup SCAN BIST

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_gpctr7_disable_fuse
registers.description: GRT 1408258877: Fuse to disable perfmon GP7. If this position changes, update BUS_UCODE_FUSE_GPCTR7_DISABLE_BIT in bnlevents.vh

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_hgs_plus_disable_fuse
registers.description: Hardware Guided Scheduler Interface Plus support. GRT 1707176000: HGS+ and HRESET support.

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_hw_sched_interface_disable_fuse
registers.description: TNT f1706661638 / b1706662797. When enabled, indicates that the HW scheduler interface is supported on this package.

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_hwp_xss_disable_fuse
registers.description: When this enable is 1, cpuid reports to software the ability to ignore HWP requests for idle processors.

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_kl_enable_fuse
registers.description: Keylocker enable fuse - VMCS PLA Color 5

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_l2_sleep_disable_timeout_bank0_fuse
registers.description: create gaps between each L2 banks' sleep disable

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_l2_sleep_disable_timeout_bank1_fuse
registers.description: create gaps between each L2 banks' sleep disable

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_l2_sleep_disable_timeout_bank2_fuse
registers.description: creat gaps between each L2 banks' sleep disable

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_l2_sleep_disable_timeout_bank3_fuse
registers.description: create gaps between each L2 banks' sleep disable

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_lam_disable_fuse
registers.description: This fuse is used to control (1=disable, 0=enable) Linear Address Masking

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_ljpll_fusecr_afs_1_afs_dd0_enable_fuse
registers.description: AFS droop detector 0 enable

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_ljpll_fusecr_afs_1_afs_dd1_enable_fuse
registers.description: AFS droop detector 1 enable

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_ljpll_fusecr_afs_1_afs_dd2_enable_fuse
registers.description: AFS droop detector 2 enable

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_ljpll_fusecr_afs_1_afs_dd3_enable_fuse
registers.description: AFS droop detector 3 enable

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_ljpll_fusecr_afs_1_afs_slowdown_enable_fuse
registers.description: PLL AFS slowdown enable; does not affect AFS droop detectors: 0x0 = disabled, 0x1 = enabled

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_ljpll_fusecr_app_0_freq_throttle_disable_ovrdval_fuse
registers.description: Interface override value for freq_throttle_disable. Encoding: 0x0 = feature enabled, 0x1 = feature disabled.

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_ljpll_fusecr_app_0_freq_throttle_ifc_ovrden_fuse
registers.description: Override enable for frequency throttle interface override values. Encoding: 0x0 = use interface value, 0x1 = use fuse override value

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_ljpll_fusecr_dcc_0_dcs_run_mode_fuse
registers.description: DCS enable and run type configuration; 0x0=disabled/bypass, 0x1=run once, 0x2=run each PLL re-lock, 0x3=run continuously

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_ljpll_fusecr_fll_perf_0_det_lock_enable_fuse
registers.description: Deterministic counter-based lock detector enable. Encoding: 0x0 = disabled (use raw lock indicator), 0x1 = enabled.

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_ljpll_fusecr_fll_perf_0_gv_afc_no_exit_on_oor_fuse
registers.description: AFC frequency search exit criteria modifier. Encoding: 0x0 = FGV exit/ack when DCO code is out-of-range, 0x1 = FGV does not exit/ack when DCO code is out-of-range.

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_ljpll_fusecr_fll_perf_0_raw_lock_count_fuse
registers.description: Frequency based lock detector duration defined as number of linear search measurements after binary search completion. Does not apply to pre-calibration or dynamic frequency changes. Encoding: 0x0 = 2, 0x1 = 4, 0x2 = 8, 0x3 = 12, 0x4 = 16, 0x5 = 24, 0x6 = 32, 0x7 = 48.

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_ljpll_fusecr_fll_proc_0_dco_ffc_disable_fuse
registers.description: DCO feed-forward coupling (FFC) disable. Encoding: 0x0 = FFC enabled, 0x1 = FFC disabled.

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_ljpll_fusecr_fll_proc_0_dco_irefres_fuse
registers.description: FLL DCO IREF bias resistance select. Encoding: 0x0 = highest current setting and higher current at hot; 0x3 = lowest current setting and higher current at cold.

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_ljpll_fusecr_fll_proc_0_dco_range_sel_fuse
registers.description: DCO current knob for higher Fmax. Encoding: 0x0 = normal frequency range, 0x1 = extended high frequency range (set automatically in OC mode).

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_ljpll_fusecr_fll_proc_0_fll_overclock_mode_fuse
registers.description: FLL overclocking (OC) mode. Encoding: 0x0 = no overclocking, 0x1 = ratio overclocking with nominal (0.5-1x) reference clock frequency, 0x2 = BCLK overclocking with elevated (1-3x) reference clock frequency, 0x3 = BCLK overclocking with extreme elevated (3-5x) reference clock frequency and ratio limited to 63.

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_ljpll_fusecr_fll_proc_0_fll_safe_mode_fuse
registers.description: FLL safe configuration mode. Encoding: 0x0 = safe mode disabled, 0x1 = safe feature settings only

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_ljpll_fusecr_perf_0_ldo2_rcomp_cfg_fuse
registers.description: LDO 2 compensation resistor configuration. Encoding: 0x0 = 1kOhm, 0x1 = 2kOhm, 0x2 = 3kOhm, 0x3 = 4kOhm.

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_ljpll_fusecr_perf_0_ldo_cbb_reserved_fuse
registers.description: Reserved for LDO CBB usage.

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_ljpll_fusecr_perf_0_misc_cbb_reserved_fuse

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_ljpll_fusecr_perf_0_shortpath_enable_fuse
registers.description: Select shortened output clock path for reduced jitter, bypassing clock throttling feauture. Encoding: 0x0 = normal output clock path, 0x1 = shortened output clock path.

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_lt_sx_en_fuse
registers.description: This fuse is used to enable/disable (1=enable, 0=disable) LT-SX feature .

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_mod_resolved_c6_enable_fuse
registers.description: Support delay C6 entry until MC6 ready when set to 1

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_module_as_1core_4thread_enable_fuse
registers.description: Enumerate an atom module as 1 core with 4 threads

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_monitorless_mwait_enable_fuse

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_msrimm_enable_fuse

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_no_punit_fuse

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pif_patch_svn_fuse
registers.description: PIF svn fuse

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pif_revid_fuse

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pif_se_svn_fuse

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_c2u_bgf_reduc1_fuse

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_c6_license_fuse

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_clock_spare_fuse
registers.description: Reserved Spare clock fuses.  Currently unused.

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_cmt_backwards_compat_fuse

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_core_dutycyclecomp_en_fuse

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_cr_spare_fuse

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_delay_stop_intfc_fuse
registers.description: FIXME Unused. Previous Usage: When set to 1, delays the stop interface from FIL to BIU by 2 UMCLKs to avoid race conditions

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_direct_not_shared_spare_fuse
registers.description: free spares

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_direct_shared_spare_fuse

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_disable_l2_sefsm_dataexp_fuse

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_dt_cycle_mclk_biu_fuse
registers.description: The duty cycle of MCLK can be adjusted based on this fuse. Negative numbers represent shortening the duty cycle, and the positive numbers represent increasing the duty cycle. By default, the duty cycle should be perfectly balanced. Note that the TAP Clock Compensation Register supports a 6-bit input that will override the fuse value. See the TNT TAP High-Level Architecture Specification for information about the TAP interface.

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_l2_voltage_mux_en_fuse
registers.description: This is Enable Fuse. If this is set, is required that the Atom Module is controlling the Module VR and not controlling the L2 VR, as controlled by their respective fuses. This may be removed if all signal toggles can be sufficiently blocked via the threshold value.

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_l2_voltage_threshold_fuse
registers.description: L2_VOLTAGE_threshold value: 11 bit threshold fuse. This is a value is milivolt/volts meaning the number programmed in this fuse will be multiplied by 2.5 mv to come up with a voltage value for the fuse. For example value 11'h000 is 0mv, 11'h001 is 2.8 mv, 11'h3ff is 5.731 volts. It Programmed to the value that the L2 voltage mux supply must switch between external Vccram supply and Vccmodule supply. It is programmed to the Vmin of the SRAM. Reset default is 0x0, signifying always select the primary supply. To disable the L2 voltage mux switching to always select the alternate supply, a value of 0x7FF can be fused.

0: No RDYSEL pushout
1: RDYSEL rise and fall pushout by 2 inversions

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_l2dat_clkshaper_wl_fuse

This fuse is used for the wordline (WL) clock shapers for l2datsubm.
X00: No WL pushout
X01: Push WL fall by 2 inversions
X10: Push WL fall by 4 inversions
X11: Push WL fall by 6 inversions

This is a wordline step underdrive setting fuse for l2datsubm.
(Bits 1 to 0)
00: No WL underdrive (read assist)
01: Weakest WL underdrive setting (read assist)
10: 2nd weakest WL underdrive setting (read assist)
11: Strongest WL underdrive setting (read assist)
(Bit 2 is no con)

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_patchable_lcp_direct_fuse
registers.description:
Speed paths detected in silicon can be corrected within the ~5% cycle time limitation of LCP. Fuse patching allows for 6 patches that can each be programmed to patch either LCP or eLCP.  17 fuses are used for either an LCP/eLCP patch. Supports 10 total patches requiring 170 fuses.  Other 4 patches are in FB_PMA_PATCHABLE_LCP_VIRTUAL_FUSE. The bit description for 17 fuses is as follows
      LCP:
      [00:00] = Valid
      [03:01] = Select CPU Partition: 3'b000 = BUS; 3'b001 = L2; 3'b010 = All cores half0 (IEC+ARR+FEC); 3'b011 = All cores half1 (MEC+FPC); 3'b100 = UCLK domain
      [04:04] = Select LCP/eLCP: LCP = 1
      [12:05] = Set LCP Address: LCP Controller grouped by 4 bit Addressing 0 - 255 (Max 256 LCP Controllers per chain)
      [14:13] = Unused
      [16:15] = DC Value: Bit 15 = DC_r; Bit 16 = DC_f
      eLCP:
      [00:00] = Valid
      [03:01] = Select CPU Partition: 3'b000 = BUS; 3'b001 = L2; 3'b010 = All cores half0 (IEC+ARR+FEC); 3'b011 = All cores half1 (MEC+FPC); 3'b100 = UCLK domain
      [04:04] = Select LCP/eLCP: eLCP = 0
      [12:05] = Set eLCP Address: eLCP Clock Domains grouped by 4 Addressing 0 - 255 (Max 1024 eLCP Clock Domains per chain)
      [13:13] = eLCP Value bit 0: eLCP Address +0 Value   (Note: 1= Enable and 0= Disable)
      [14:14] = eLCP Value bit 1: eLCP Address +1 Value   (Note: 1= Enable and 0= Disable)
      [15:15] = eLCP Value bit 2: eLCP Address +2 Value   (Note: 1= Enable and 0= Disable)
      [16:16] = eLCP Value bit 3: eLCP Address +3 Value   (Note: 1= Enable and 0= Disable)

registers.name: atom_fuse_fb_pma_uclk_dcc16_fuse
registers.description:
Adjustable delay line setting on ckuclksocscan_fu1nxx (uclk) TNT has 4 critical input clocks with synchronous paths between them.  Each of these 4 clocks also has its own usync signals indicating its global alignment edge.  Because the relative clock arrival times are determined by SOC, we plan to implement an adjustable delay line on each of the 4 critical clocks and associated usyncs.  The Adjustable delay, controlled by 4 bit fuse, could produce delay as short as 1 buffer and as long as 16 buffers.
The default values for the adjustable delay lines are all 0000 which indicates minimum delay which is 1 buffer.  (1111 is the maximum delay setting which is 16 buffers)

registers.name: fw_fuses_atom_core_dlvr_supported
registers.description:
Allows for specific DLVR (Atom on ring) to enter in regulation. (1= enable, 0 = disable).
              If this OR result is 0 (disable), the DLVR will be in constant bypass mode (never regulated, might be off).
              Default is 0 (disabled).

registers.name: fw_fuses_cluster0_atom_leak_params_eq_vt
registers.description:
Leakage equation correction VT.
                         Correction for the leakage temperature multiplier in the equation e^(aV+bT-cVT)
                         Important note: It is burnt as positive number and Pcode is subtracting.

                Used for WA for HSD 14022432688.

Should be 0 in client products and E3, 1 in server and IOTG/FuSa products

registers.name: fw_fuses_cluster0_bigcore_itd_floor_v
registers.description: ITD correction for voltages below FLOOR_V will be the same as for FLOOR_V. See RPL HSD 1309935722

registers.name: fw_fuses_cluster0_ccp_1_ia_p0_ratio_downbin
registers.description:
Determines max ratio for this core by subtracting this value from IA_P0_RATIO.
                This is part of Favored Core Turbo (FCT) feature.
                As this is Turbo feature, the result P0 (IA_P0_RATIO - this fuse) must still be at least P1 (IA_P1_RATIO)

registers.name: fw_fuses_cluster0_ccp_2_ia_cdyn_var
registers.description:
Correction factor on top of IA_CDYN_MAX fuse, for this specific core.
                         This core Cdyn = IA_CDYN_MAX * this_fuse.
                         Values supported are between 0.0 and almost 2.0 of the nominal value.

registers.name: fw_fuses_cluster0_ccp_2_ia_leak_var
registers.description:
Correction factor on top of IA_LEAK fuse, for this specific core.
                         This core Leak = IA_LEAK * this_fuse.
                         Values supported are between 0.0 and almost 2.0 of the nominal value.

registers.name: fw_fuses_cluster0_ccp_2_ia_p0_ratio_downbin
registers.description:
Determines max ratio for this core by subtracting this value from IA_P0_RATIO.
                This is part of Favored Core Turbo (FCT) feature.
                As this is Turbo feature, the result P0 (IA_P0_RATIO - this fuse) must still be at least P1 (IA_P1_RATIO)

registers.name: fw_fuses_cluster0_ia_phy_core_disable_mask
registers.description:
Disabling individual cores. 4 bits per physical CCP. Each bit represents core in the CCP. Total of 8 supported CCP in this cluster. Not changing physical to logical mapping. BigCore is using only bit 0 of its CCP, Atom is using all 4 bits of its CCP.
              Examples:
              Disabling BigCore0 : 0x0001
              Disabling BigCore0 and BigCore1 : 0x0011
              Disabling BigCore3 only : 0x1000
              PTL example:
              Disable BigCore0, Atom-CCP7-Core1 : 0x2000001

registers.name: fw_fuses_cluster1_ia_phy_core_disable_mask
registers.description:
Disabling individual cores. 4 bits per physical CCP. Each bit represents core in the CCP. Total of 1 supported CCP in this cluster. Not changing physical to logical mapping. BigCore is using only bit 0 of its CCP, Atom is using all 4 bits of its CCP.
              Examples:
              Disabling Atom0 : 0x1 (0b0001)
              Disabling Atom0 and Atom1 : 0x3 (0b0011)
              Disabling Atom3 only : 0x8 (0b1000)

registers.description: Tight loop lock mode switch latency - wait time in refclk cycles after enabling distribution clock before switching to long loop feedback. Encoding: 0x0=3 cycles, 0x1=7 cycles.

registers.name: auxpll_fuse_fusecr_app_0_tog_clkout_parking
registers.description: Toggle output clock park state to counter aging effects; 0x0=output clock parks low, 0x1=alternate output clock parking level

registers.name: auxpll_fuse_fusecr_dcc_0_dcs_run_mode
registers.description: DCS enable and run type configuration; 0x0=disabled/bypass, 0x1=run once, 0x2=run each PLL re-lock, 0x3=run continuously

registers.name: auxpll_fuse_fusecr_perf_0_ldo2_rcomp_cfg
registers.description: LDO 2 compensation resistor configuration. Encoding: 0x0 = 1kOhm, 0x1 = 2kOhm, 0x2 = 3kOhm, 0x3 = 4kOhm.

registers.name: auxpll_fuse_fusecr_perf_0_ldo_cbb_reserved
registers.description: Reserved for LDO CBB usage.

registers.name: auxpll_fuse_fusecr_perf_0_misc_cbb_reserved
registers.description: Reserved for future use

registers.name: auxpll_fuse_fusecr_perf_0_shortpath_enable

registers.name: auxpll_fuse_fusecr_proc_0_fast_lock_higain_disable
registers.description: Fast-lock high-gain during lock acquisition feature disable; 0x0=feature enabled, 0x1=feature disabled

registers.name: auxpll_fuse_fusecr_proc_0_pvd_mode
registers.description: Static Post-VCO Divider setting; 0x0 = div-1; 0x1 = div-2; 0x2 = div-4; 0x3 = div-8

DFSM, 0x51000[26]

Must be 0 on all parts, except if 1 is requested for inidividual parts for special debug.

registers.name: de_hdcp_dis
registers.description:
SKUing/Security: Disable HDCP capability
DFSM, 0x51000[25]

registers.name: de_kvmr_capture_dis

registers.name: de_multi_chip_genlock_dis
registers.description:
This field when set will disable multi-chip genlock functionality on both primary and secondary genlock systems.
Single chip synchronization use cases are not impacted by this fuse.
DSSM, 0x51004[0]

registers.name: de_pavp_wakelock_dis
registers.description:
DFSM[7]
This bit disables the wake lock logic for PAVP connection state and register programming. When disabled, display hardware will not automatically wake from DC5 for PAVP programming, so DC5 will need to be disabled when using PAVP.

registers.name: de_pipea_dis

registers.name: de_pipec_dis
registers.description:
SKUing: Disable pipe C capability
DFSM, 0x51000[28]

registers.name: de_retention_dis
registers.description: Security: This bit indicates whether display retention capability is disabled or enabled. When retention is enabled, display PAVP save and restore capability is blocked and PAVP state is preserved with retention. When retention is disabled, display PAVP save and restore capability is allowed. DFSM[5]

registers.name: de_rsb_dis
registers.description:
SKUing: Disable Remote Screen Blanking capability
DFSM, 0X51000[6]

registers.name: de_trim_hdspsram_stbyp
registers.description:
Debug mode
[0]  -  Self time (default)
[1]  -  Burn-in synchronous mode

registers.name: de_trim_hdspsram_wa_disable

Enables/disables write-assist circuit.
[0] - Enables write-assist circuit
[1] - Disables write-assist circuit

registers.name: de_trim_hdspsram_wpulse
registers.description:
Controls delay between WL and Negative Bit Line (NBL) pulse turning on for write-assist.
[01] - Gives shortest delay
[00] - Gives 2nd shortest delay
[10] - Gives 2nd longest delay
[11] - Gives longest delay

registers.description: Frequency based lock detector duration defined as number of linear search measurements after binary search completion. Does not apply to pre-calibration or dynamic frequency changes. Encoding: 0x0 = 2, 0x1 = 4, 0x2 = 8, 0x3 = 12, 0x4 = 16, 0x5 = 24, 0x6 = 32, 0x7 = 48.

registers.name: dfdpll_top_fusecr_fll_proc_0_dco_range_sel
registers.description: DCO current knob for higher Fmax. Encoding: 0x0 = normal frequency range, 0x1 = extended high frequency range (set automatically in OC mode).

registers.name: dfdpll_top_fusecr_fll_proc_0_fll_overclock_mode
registers.description: FLL overclocking (OC) mode. Encoding: 0x0 = no overclocking, 0x1 = ratio overclocking with nominal (0.5-1x) reference clock frequency, 0x2 = BCLK overclocking with elevated (1-3x) reference clock frequency, 0x3 = BCLK overclocking with extreme elevated (3-5x) reference clock frequency and ratio limited to 63.

registers.name: dfdpll_top_fusecr_fll_proc_0_fll_safe_mode
registers.description: FLL safe configuration mode. Encoding: 0x0 = safe mode disabled, 0x1 = safe feature settings only

registers.name: dfdpll_top_fusecr_perf_0_misc_cbb_reserved

registers.name: dfdpll_top_fusecr_perf_0_shortpath_enable

registers.name: dfdpll_top_fusecr_proc_0_ldo_vrefsel

registers.name: dfdpll_top_fusecr_proc_0_pvd_mode

registers.name: fw_fuses_bclk_oc_range
registers.description:
The allowed range for BCLK frequency overclocking.
            Used by BCLK-governor (in case it is enabled, see fuse BCLK_GOV_EN)
            Bclk frequency is measured w.r.t. PciBCLK (which assumed to be 100Mhz)

registers.name: fw_fuses_boot_voltage_guardband
registers.description:
Defines early reset voltage guardband, before BIOS can program VR load-line and so.
            Also required for survivability, as patch was not loaded at this point.
            It cannot be a fixed boot voltage as in legacy products, as VR topology is unknown at fusing,
            and as boot freq is vary.
            The recommended value is around 100mV, sufficient to cover for load-line and survivability,
            but still should be within the normal dynamic voltage of all products.

registers.name: fw_fuses_ddr_oc_en
registers.description:
This fuse is used to resolve CAPID0_A bit 3 DDR OC.
            The formula of the CAPID field is:
              If DDR_OC_EN = 1 -> CAPID DDR_OC_EN = 1
              If DSKU override for DDR OC = 1 -> CAPID DDR_OC_EN = 1

registers.name: fw_fuses_ddrsz
registers.description:
DDR Size per Channel
                Field defines the maximum allowed memory size per MC channel
                * 00: Unlimited (64 GB per channel)
                * 01: Maximum 8GB per channel
                * 10: Maximum 4GB per channel
                * 11: Maximum 2GB per channel
                For DDR5 this is the channel size
                For LPDDR4/5 this is the total size of 2 subchannels.

                If ECC_EXIST = 0, force CAPID0_A.FDEE = 0 and CAPID0_A.ECCDIS = 1. If ECC_EXIST = 1, unchanged.

registers.name: fw_fuses_igd
registers.description:
Internal Graphics Disable.
            The fuse is being used in resolving with DSKU.
                  0 : There is a graphics engine within this CPU.
                      Internal Graphics Device (Device 2) is enabled and all of its memory and I/O spaces are accessable.
                      Configuration cycles to Device 2 will be completed within the CPU.
                      All non-SMM memory and IO accesses to VGA will be handled based on Memory and IO enables of Device 2 and IO registers within Device 2 and VGA Enable of the PCI to PCI bridge control (If PCI Express GFX attach is supported). A selected amount of Graphics Memory space is pre-allocated from the main memory based on Graphics Mode Select (GMS in the GGC Register). Graphics Memory is pre-allocated above TSEG Memory.
                  1 : There is no graphics engine within this CPU.
                      Internal Graphics Device (Device 2) and all of its memory and I/O functions are disabled.
                      Configuration cycle targeted to Device 2 will be passed on.
                      All non-SMM memory and IO accesses to VGA will be handled based on VGA Enable of the PCI to PCI bridge control.
                      Device 2 is disabled and hidden.
            The CAPID0_A IGD field {internal GFX disabled} = 1, if (IGD fuse = 1 or IGD DSKU = 1), else 0 .

registers.name: fw_fuses_max_f_lpddr5

registers.name: fw_fuses_mc0_power_gating_disable

ADL-B0 new behavior: This fuse disables MC0 reset and power gating. They are coupled.
                If MC0 slice is not occupied pcode will logicaly reset it and electrically power gate it, unless this fuse is set.

registers.name: fw_fuses_memss_rest_cdyn_conductivity_max

In addition to QCLK we have other (e.g. SBclk) domains, as well as complex mixed domains,
            All these 'rest' should be put here.
            Note, the conductivity units are the same as Cdyn[nF]@Ghz!, Scaling of current with voltage in U9.3.6 mho(1/ohm) for MEMSS domain.

                resolution. Up to 127 Watts. 0x00 means no power limits.

registers.name: fw_fuses_oc_bins
registers.description:
OC_BINS contains how many bits of over-clocking are supported.
                0 - unsupported amp; cannot be enabled using dynamic sku.
                1..6 - max 1..6 bin of overclocking is supported.
                7 - unlimited OC is supported.

registers.name: fw_fuses_oc_enabled
registers.description:
OC_ENABLED is 1 if over-clocking is enabled, 0 if not.
            Corresponds to CAPID OC_ENABLE_DSKU, when the VDM sets this bit, we will apply OC if FUSE_OC_ENABLED_DSKU = '1

              Mask can disable multiple PkgC states

registers.description:
GadiM: this is SVM disabled. It defined as SPARE24 in pcode strap files, although it should be SVMDIS.
            But it is distributed correctly (see CAPID in DE, which uses this bit).

registers.name: fw_fuses_telemetry_aggr_dis
registers.description: Complete disable of Telemetry aggregator and 'sub features'-sampler and tracer.

registers.name: fw_fuses_temp_target
registers.description:
Fan Temperature target offset(T-Control). This field describes the negative offset from
                the fused Tj-max at which customers should engage fans in order to meet Intel thermal
                reliability specs.  LSB is 2'C. Data is unsigned, negative is implied (U4.5.-1)

registers.name: fw_fuses_timed_mwait_disable
registers.description: Fuse when set will disable the Timed MWAIT feature (HSW B-step ECO 4244601)

registers.name: fw_fuses_turbo_disable
registers.description: turbo disable

registers.name: fw_fuses_turbo_time_window
registers.description:
Programmable clipping threshold for RAPL limits max time window size.  A value of zero
                (default value) means no clipping (time window will be limited only to 448 seconds, the
                maximum supported by pcode).  The format of this number is a 5-bit unsigned number which
                is an exponent, units are 1/1024 seconds (same as RAPL time window units).  For example a
                value of 10 here means a time window of 2^10 * 1/1024 seconds = 1 second.  A better name
                for this fuse would be RAPL_MAX_TIME_WINDOW.

registers.name: fw_fuses_vtdd
registers.description:
VT-d Capability - I/O Virtualization (VT for DMA)
      0: Enable VTd
      1: Disable VTd

registers.description: This fuse is used in order to disable the raising of a mca when double exception error occurs.

registers.name: punit_fuses_disable_dram_double_ecc_err_ind
registers.description: This fuse is used in order to disable the raising of a mca when dram double ecc error occurs.

registers.name: punit_fuses_disable_halt_ecc_double_err
registers.description: This fuse is used in order to disable halting uC when there is ecc double error indication from iram or sram

registers.name: punit_fuses_fusa_bist_parallel
registers.description: This is a chickenbit, to revert FUSA BIST back to LNL parallel method. In PTL we moved to quasi parallel dividing the flow to 4 serial stages. 1'b0 - default - PTL style. 1'b1 - revert to LNL style.

registers.name: punit_fuses_hw_fuse_spare0
registers.description: Spare Punit HW Fuse

registers.name: punit_fuses_hw_fuse_spare1
registers.description: Spare Punit HW Fuse

registers.name: punit_fuses_hw_fuse_spare2
registers.description: Spare Punit HW Fuses

registers.name: punit_fuses_sram_init_parallel
registers.description: This is a chickenbit, to revert SRAM INIT back to LNL parallel method. In PTL we moved to quasi parallel dividing the flow to 4 serial stages. 1'b0 - default - PTL style. 1'b1 - revert to LNL style.

registers.name: punit_fuses_ssa_hw_fuses_ra
registers.description: Read assist level control bus. Controls WL voltage droop during Read Assist

registers.name: punit_fuses_ssa_hw_fuses_sbc
registers.description: Sleep bias level control bus for when deepsleep=1. Used to program sleep strength to control array retention supply voltage

registers.name: punit_fuses_ssa_hw_fuses_stbyp
registers.description: Overrides self-timed clocking to support debug. Forces the read to occur at falling edge of clock clk

registers.name: punit_fuses_ssa_hw_fuses_wa_disable
registers.description: Disables write-assist circuit

registers.name: punit_fuses_ssa_hw_fuses_wpulse
registers.description: Write pulse duration control bus. Controls delay between WL and Negative Bit Line (NBL) pulse turning on for write-assist

registers.name: qclkpll_fuse_fusecr_app_0_pll_safe_mode
registers.description: PLL safe configuration mode. Encoding: 0x0 = safe mode disabled, 0x1 = safe feature settings only, 0x2-0x3 = reserved.

registers.name: qclkpll_fuse_fusecr_app_0_tllm_switch_latency
registers.description: Tight loop lock mode switch latency - wait time in refclk cycles after enabling distribution clock before switching to long loop feedback. Encoding: 0x0=3 cycles, 0x1=7 cycles.

registers.name: qclkpll_fuse_fusecr_app_0_tog_clkout_parking
registers.description: Toggle output clock park state to counter aging effects; 0x0=output clock parks low, 0x1=alternate output clock parking level

registers.name: qclkpll_fuse_fusecr_dcc_0_dcs_run_mode
registers.description: DCS enable and run type configuration; 0x0=disabled/bypass, 0x1=run once, 0x2=run each PLL re-lock, 0x3=run continuously

registers.name: qclkpll_fuse_fusecr_perf_0_ldo2_rcomp_cfg
registers.description: LDO 2 compensation resistor configuration. Encoding: 0x0 = 1kOhm, 0x1 = 2kOhm, 0x2 = 3kOhm, 0x3 = 4kOhm.

registers.name: qclkpll_fuse_fusecr_perf_0_ldo_cbb_reserved
registers.description: Reserved for LDO CBB usage.

registers.name: qclkpll_fuse_fusecr_perf_0_misc_cbb_reserved
registers.description: Reserved for future use

registers.name: qclkpll_fuse_fusecr_proc_0_fast_lock_higain_disable
registers.description: Fast-lock high-gain during lock acquisition feature disable; 0x0=feature enabled, 0x1=feature disabled

registers.name: qclkpll_fuse_fusecr_proc_0_pvd_mode
registers.description: Static Post-VCO Divider setting; 0x0 = div-1; 0x1 = div-2; 0x2 = div-4; 0x3 = div-8

registers.name: qclkpll_fuse_fusecr_proc_0_pvd_threshold
registers.description: Dynamic (ratio based) Post-VCO Divider setting; 0x0 = use static setting from pvd_mode fuse; 0x1 - 0x3F = binary value of ratio at and below which div-2/4/8 is enabled

registers.name: qclkpll_fuse_fusecr_proc_0_startup_rdac_hifreq_thresh
registers.description: Output clock frequency threshold above which to use 'highfreq' startup rdac setting. Encoding: 0x1 = 1.6GHz, 0x1 = 2.0GHz.

registers.name: qclkpll_fuse_fusecr_proc_1_ldo_vrefgen_sel
registers.description: LDO Vref source selection; 0x0=external BG-based VREF (bgvref_a), 0x1=internal RDAC-based Vref from vccana_ehv

registers.name: qclkpll_fuse_fusecr_proc_1_pwrup_clk_ovrd
registers.description: Power-up clock select interface strap override. Encoding: 0x0 = no override, 0x1 = bypass clock, 0x2 = reference clock, 0x3 = legacy RO clock.

registers.name: qclkpll_fuse_fusecr_proc_1_reserved3
registers.description: Reserved for future use

registers.name: qclkpll_fuse_fusecr_proc_1_ro_freq_sel
registers.description: Contention clearing ring oscillator frequency select. Encoding: 0x0 = fastest (100-800MHz), 0x1 = faster (80-400MHz), 0x2 = slower (50-280MHz), 0x3 = slowest (40-200MHz) (across 0.65V-1.1V, rcss-rcff)
